title: The Kiss Thief
author: L.J. Shen
synopsis: Francesca Rossi is promised to Angelo Bandini, heir to a powerful Chicago Outfit family. But when ruthless Senator Wolfe Keaton steals her first kiss and forces her into marriage using her father's sins, she finds herself torn between two rivals fighting for her heart — and her forever.

title: The Ruthless King
author: Maya Hughes
synopsis: Emmett and Avery were high school sweethearts until secrets and circumstances drove them apart. Four years later, she's back in his life and he's determined that everything will be on his terms — but being this close makes him question everything he thought he knew about her. A second-chance, enemies-to-lovers sports romance.

title: Chasing The Fierce Heiress
author: Unknown
synopsis: A fiery first encounter sparks an unforgettable clash when Hazel's bold spirit captivates Joshua, a ruthless billionaire with a penchant for control. To ensnare her, Joshua orchestrates a daring surrogacy competition, forcing her into his world.

title: It Ends With Us
author: Colleen Hoover
synopsis: Lily hasn't always had it easy, but that's never stopped her from working hard for the life she wants. When she feels a spark with a gorgeous neurosurgeon named Ryle Kincaid, everything in Lily's life suddenly seems almost too good to be true.

title: It Starts With Us
author: Colleen Hoover
synopsis: Lily and her ex-husband, Ryle, have just settled into a civil co-parenting rhythm when she suddenly runs into her first love, Atlas, again. After nearly two years separated, she is elated that for once, time is on their side.

title: A Million Kisses In Your Lifetime
author: Monica Murphy
synopsis: Wren Beaumont is a model student loved by everyone but brooding campus bad boy Crew Lancaster. When their lives unexpectedly collide, Wren discovers there's more to life than good grades.

title: Fifty Shades of Grey
author: E.L. James
synopsis: When literature student Anastasia Steele interviews young entrepreneur Christian Grey, she encounters a man who is beautiful, brilliant, and intimidating. Unable to resist Ana's quiet beauty, Grey admits he wants her, too—but on his own terms.

title: Fifty Shades Darker
author: E.L. James
synopsis: Daunted by the dark secrets of Christian Grey, Anastasia Steele breaks off their relationship. But desire still dominates her every thought, and when he proposes a new arrangement, she cannot resist.

title: Fifty Shades Freed
author: E.L. James
synopsis: Shocked and intrigued by Christian's singular erotic tastes, Ana demands a deeper commitment. Determined to keep her, Christian agrees. Now, Ana and Christian have it all—love, passion, intimacy, and wealth.

title: The Love Hypothesis
author: Ali Hazelwood
synopsis: Olive Smith doesn't believe in lasting romantic relationships. To convince her best friend she is dating, she panics and kisses the first man she sees: Adam Carlsen, a young hotshot professor and well-known ass.

title: The Spanish Love Deception
author: Elena Armas
synopsis: Catalina Martín needs a date to her sister's wedding to sustain a lie about her American boyfriend. Her condescending colleague, Aaron Blackford, surprisingly offers to step in.

title: The American Roommate Experiment
author: Elena Armas
synopsis: Rosie Graham has writer's block and a crumbled ceiling. She ends up sharing her friend's apartment with Lucas, who proposes a series of experimental dates to jump-start her romantic inspiration.

title: Twisted Love
author: Ana Huang
synopsis: Alex Volkov is a devil with the face of an angel and a past he can't escape. When forced to look after his best friend's sister, Ava Chen, he starts to feel a fire that could end his world.

title: Twisted Games
author: Ana Huang
synopsis: Elite bodyguard Rhys Larsen has two rules: protect his clients and never get emotionally involved. He's never been tempted to break them until Bridget von Ascheberg, a princess with a hidden fire.

title: Shatter Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Juliette hasn't touched anyone in 264 days because her touch is fatal. The Reestablishment has locked her up, but now they think she might be exactly the weapon they need.

title: Unravel Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Juliette and Adam escape to join a group of rebels. She struggles with her deadly power and her confusing connection to Warner, who seems immune to her touch.

title: Ignite Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: With Omega Point destroyed, Juliette must rely on Warner to master her powers and take down The Reestablishment once and for all. But Warner wants more than just a victory.

title: Restore Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Juliette is now the Supreme Commander of Sector 45. She has the whole world in her hand, but when tragedy hits, she must decide who she will become.

title: Defy Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Juliette's tenure as leader is a disaster. Warner reveals secrets about her family and identity that change everything she thought she knew about herself.

title: Imagine Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Ella knows who Juliette is and what she was created for. As she struggles with her past, the lines between Ella and Juliette blur while old enemies loom.

title: Believe Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Life in the aftermath of The Reestablishment is hard. While stabilizing the world, Warner is eager to finally marry Juliette, the person he loves most.

title: Destroy Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Warner must suppress a rebellion while obsessed with finding Juliette. When his father arrives, he realizes there are much different plans for her future.

title: I Like You Like That
author: Kayla Tirrell
synopsis: Famous rock star Birdie Wilder is reunited with her teenage rejection, Liam Miller, who is hired as her bodyguard to protect her from a stalker.

title: Beautiful Player
author: Christina Lauren
synopsis: Hanna Bergstrom turns to her brother's best friend, Will Sumner, to help her start dating. But a wild night teaches Will he can't forget her.

title: King Of Pride
author: Ana Huang
synopsis: Kai Young is controlled and proper until he meets Isabella, who is chaos personified. He is tempted to break all his rules to claim her as his own.

title: Final Offer
author: Lauren Asher
synopsis: Callahan Kane must spend a summer at the family lake house to claim his inheritance, putting him face-to-face with Alana, the only woman he ever loved.

title: Unfortunately Yours
author: Tessa Bailey
synopsis: Natalie Vos needs to be married to access her trust fund. She strikes a deal with August Cates for a marriage of convenience that turns into something dangerous.

title: Terms and Conditions
author: Lauren Asher
synopsis: Declan Kane must marry to become CEO. He enters a marriage of convenience with his assistant Iris, only to realize she was never just a transaction.

title: The Fine Print
author: Lauren Asher
synopsis: Rowan Kane encounters Zahra Gulian while renovating Dreamland. Despite the 'fine print' of their professional relationship, he finds himself falling for her.

title: King of Wrath
author: Ana Huang
synopsis: Dante Russo is blackmailed into an engagement with Vivian Lau. He's determined to end it, but find himself obsessed with the woman he never intended to keep.

title: Twisted Hate
author: Ana Huang
synopsis: Josh Chen and Jules Ambrose enter a 'friends with benefits' arrangement with one rule: no falling in love. Their hatred soon turns into a fire neither can control.

title: Twisted Lies
author: Ana Huang
synopsis: Christian Harper steps in as Stella Alonso's fake boyfriend for protection. Stella soon realizes the man protecting her might be the most dangerous threat of all.

title: The Maddest Obsession
author: Danielle Lori
synopsis: Christian Allister, a cold FBI agent, is dangerously obsessed with Gianna, the wife of an enemy. Their connection threatens to unravel his carefully constructed world.

title: Corrupted Chaos
author: Shain Rose
synopsis: Izzy has earned her spot on a cybersecurity team, but her new boss Cade Armanelli—billionaire hacker and antisocial loner—is enemy number one. Forced into a single cabin and bed during a work retreat, their war of wills turns explosive. He's a distraction she can't afford, and she's a spitfire he can't resist.

title: Archer's Voice
author: Mia Sheridan
synopsis: Bree Prescott seeks peace in Maine and meets Archer Hale, an isolated man holding a secret agony. It is a story of memory, trauma, and finding freedom.

title: Black Ties and White Lies
author: Kat Singleton
synopsis: Margo lands a job as a fake fiancée for a billionaire. The white lies they tell the public start to feel real, revealing her boss's hidden heart of gold.

title: Bound by Honour
author: Cora Reilly
synopsis: Aria Scuderi is betrothed to Luca Vitiello to ensure peace between mafia families. She is terrified of marrying a man known as 'The Vice.'

title: Fix Her Up
author: Tessa Bailey
synopsis: Georgie Castle enlists her brother's best friend, Travis Ford, for a fake relationship to gain respect. Their renovated love life soon feels permanent.

title: Owned by a Sinner
author: Michelle Heard
synopsis: Sold to Liam O'Shea to settle a debt, I'm thrust into the heart of the Irish mob. The man who 'owns' me might be the only one who can truly set me free.

title: Priest
author: Sierra Simone
synopsis: Father Tyler Bell has always followed the rules until Poppy confesses. She is his temptation, testing his vows and making him want to sin.

title: The Tease
author: Monica Murphy
synopsis: Jace uses his charm to get what he wants until he meets Maya, who is immune. It's a game of cat and mouse for a love neither expected.

title: The Off-Limits Rule
author: Sarah Adams
synopsis: Cooper is my brother's best friend and completely off-limits. But when we're forced to live together, the lines between hate and desire blur.

title: The Sweetest Oblivion
author: Danielle Lori
synopsis: Elena Abelli is forced into an arrangement with Nicolas Russo. The oblivion he offers her is more sweet than she ever could have imagined.

title: Corrupted By You
author: Unknown
synopsis: Elara is trying to rebuild her life until she meets Silas, who is the definition of trouble. He is determined to draw Elara out of her shell.

title: Fracture Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Told from Adam's perspective, he is torn between duty to the resistance and his need to protect his brother while struggling with his feelings for Juliette.

title: Shadow Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Kenji struggles to balance his friendship with Juliette and his duties as a leader, while dealing with the feeling of living in the shadows.

title: Reveal Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
synopsis: Picking up after Defy Me, Kenji must keep his friends together and figure out their next move as they face their greatest challenge yet.

title: Haunting Adeline
author: H.D. Carlton
synopsis: I can manipulate emotions, but my words don't affect the Shadow. He's always there, watching and waiting, and I can never look away.

title: Hunting Adeline
author: H.D. Carlton
synopsis: I'm trapped in a world full of monsters. The Hunter will burn the world down to get me back and carve his name into the souls of anyone who stops him.

title: King of Greed
author: Ana Huang
synopsis: Dominic Davenport's obsession with success has pushed his wife Alessandra away. He'll do anything to win her back, but she is done being second to his empire.

title: Unexpectedly Mine
author: Erin Hawkins
synopsis: I've been the 'boring' sister until a rugged stranger named Crew comes to town. We were supposed to be just a fling, but he's become unexpectedly mine.

title: A Little Twist
author: Unknown
synopsis: Rafe and Lily have been best friends for years until a drunken wedding in Vegas creates a 'little twist' in their relationship.

title: Too Beautiful To Break
author: Tessa Layne
synopsis: Pax Tate is an arrogant asshole until he meets Mila Hill, the only person who sees through his act to the broken man underneath.

title: Den of Vipers
author: K.A. Knight
synopsis: My father gambles me away to settle a debt with the Vipers. In a den of monsters, I'm the one they should be afraid of.

title: Moments of Malevolence
author: Unknown
synopsis: I've lived in the shadows waiting for the right moment to strike. It's a dark dance of obsession and revenge with the woman who challenges me.

title: Redeemed
author: Lauren Asher
synopsis: Santiago Alatorre is a racer searching for redemption. He and Maya find a connection in their shared pain and the strength to love again.

title: Collided
author: Lauren Asher
synopsis: Liam and Sophie have been rivals since they were kids. Forced to work together, their mutual hatred turns into an electric attraction.

title: Throttled
author: Lauren Asher
synopsis: Maya Alatorre wants to stay away from her brother's rival, Noah Slade. But the chemistry pulling them together on the racing circuit is hard to ignore.

title: Wrecked
author: Lauren Asher
synopsis: Jax and Elena are two people wrecked by their pasts who find common ground in their brokenness in a small coastal town.

title: Now You're Mine
author: Unknown
synopsis: He's been watching me and decided that I belong to him. Now that he's claimed me, there's no turning back. I'm his, and he's mine.

title: Sinner
author: Sierra Simone
synopsis: Liam Brennan lives by his own rules. As a priest, he meets Tessa and finds himself questioning everything he's ever believed in.

title: The Highest Bidder
author: Unknown
synopsis: Gabriella is sold to the highest bidder to save her family. Julian's intentions are far more complex than she ever imagined.

title: Bound By Honor
author: Cora Reilly
synopsis: Aria Scuderi is eighteen and the day she dreaded is arriving: her marriage to Luca Vitiello, the future Capo of the New York Famiglia.

title: Ryan Rule
author: Sadie Kincaid
synopsis: Jessie meets the four Ryan brothers who share everything. She must decide if she's willing to break all her rules to be with them.

title: Ryan Redemption
author: Sadie Kincaid
synopsis: The brothers and Jessie face new challenges and ghosts from the past. It's a journey of redemption to protect their unconventional love.

title: Ryan Retribution
author: Sadie Kincaid
synopsis: In the final installment, the Ryan brothers and Jessie are out for blood and revenge after a devastating betrayal.

title: Ryan Reign
author: Sadie Kincaid
synopsis: The Ryan brothers and Jessie claim their throne, but reigning over the underworld is about survival. They must stand united or watch their empire crumble.

title: Ryan Renewed
author: Sadie Kincaid
synopsis: Jessie and the brothers must find a way to live in peace, but peace is fragile for men born of violence. They must decide their legacy.

title: Ryan Recollection
author: Sadie Kincaid
synopsis: A collection of memories and lost scenes offering a deeper look at the Ryan brothers and the woman who changed everything.

title: Cruel King
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Levi King is the king of Royal Elite Academy. Astrid Clifford is the only one who doesn't bow to his crown, uncovering a darkness that links their pasts.

title: Deviant King
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Aiden King decides Elsa Steel belongs to him. He doesn't just want her body; he wants to break her spirit using her forgotten past.

title: Steel Princess
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Elsa fights to regain her memories. She realizes that the 'king' she feared might be the only one who can protect her from true monsters.

title: Twisted Kingdom
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Aiden and Elsa must decide if their twisted love is enough to see them through the wreckage of devastating truths about their past.

title: Black Knight
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Xander Knight hides a secret that is killing him. Kimberly Reed has always loved him, and they must face the truth together or be claimed by darkness.

title: Vicious Prince
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Ronan Astor and Teal van Dieren are forced into a marriage of convenience. Their friction turns into a fire that might be their most dangerous secret.

title: Ruthless Empire
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Cole Nash sets out to break Silver Queensley, but realizes she might be the only person in the world who is just as ruthless as he is.

title: God of Malice
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Killian Carson has no mercy. He decides Glyndon King is his plaything, but he might find she's the only one who can handle his chaos.

title: God of Pain
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Creighton King lives in a world of silence until he meets Annika Volkov. Her resilience challenges the walls around his heart.

title: God of Wrath
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Jeremy Volkov is the 'God of Wrath.' His connection with Cecily Knight is explosive and forbidden, fueled by a wrath that threatens everyone.

title: God of Ruin
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Landon King sets out to ruin Mia Sokolov's life for fun, but realizes she is a survivor who is ready to play his dangerous games.

title: God of Fury
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Brandon King, a disciplined artist and athlete, finds his carefully controlled life shattered when he becomes the target of Nikolai Sokolov’s dangerous obsession. As the volatile Russian mafia heir pushes Brandon to his limits, a dark, forbidden connection ignites that challenges their identities and family loyalties.

title: God of War
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Ava Nash wakes up in a hospital with two years of missing memories, only to discover she is married to Eli King—the ruthless man she has loathed since childhood. Trapped in a marriage of convenience with a husband who refuses to let her go, she must uncover the dark secrets of her past and the truth behind their union.

title: Captured Solace
author: Unknown
synopsis: Hazel's life takes a dark turn when she is kidnapped by Joshua, a man claiming to be her protector. As she navigates her new reality, she finds solace in his arms, but soon realizes his love comes with a heavy price.

title: A Long Time Coming
author: Meghan Quinn
synopsis: Breanna and Ty have been best friends since childhood, but a drunken night of passion changes everything. Now, they must navigate the complexities of their new relationship while dealing with the fallout of their pasts.

title: Flawless
author: Elsie Silver
synopsis: Rhett Eaton is the golden boy of professional bull riding — until a public brawl and viral controversy cost him his sponsors. Enter Summer Hamilton, his agent's sharp-tongued daughter, who is stuck babysitting him for the rest of the season. He's arrogant, she's uptight, and somewhere between forced road trips and late-night heart-to-hearts, the line between enemies and something more starts to blur.

title: Heartless
author: Elsie Silver
synopsis: Working as a nanny for the world's grumpiest single dad should have been simple. But Willa Grant can't keep her eyes off brooding rancher Cade Eaton, and after a game of truth or dare in the hot tub, all bets are off. He softens in their quiet moments — and it turns out broad-shouldered cowboys with calloused hands are this city girl's kryptonite.

title: Powerless
author: Lauren Roberts
synopsis: In the kingdom of Ilya, being born without a power is a crime. Ordinary-born Paedyn Gray has survived by posing as a Psychic — until she saves a prince and is thrown into the brutal Purging Trials. If the competition doesn't kill her, the prince she's fighting feelings for will, once he discovers what she truly is.

title: The Predator
author: RuNyx
synopsis: Tristan Caine is the only non-blooded member of the ruthless Tenebrae Outfit — lethal, enigmatic, and feared. When Morana Vitalio, genius daughter of a rival mafia family, infiltrates his home to prove his guilt in a decades-old mystery, they discover a secret that binds them together. Hate, heat, and history collide in this dark, enemies-to-lovers mafia romance.

title: The Reaper
author: RuNyx
synopsis: Continuing Tristan Caine and Morana Vitalio's story, Morana is now in enemy territory with Tristan as her unlikely protector. As they hunt for the truth together and skeletons are uncovered, an anonymous figure known only as 'The Reaper' begins sending Morana cryptic information that could change everything.

title: Vicious
author: L.J. Shen
synopsis: Baron 'Vicious' Spencer is brilliant, ruthless, and obsessed with Emilia LeBlanc — the girl he bullied and drove away ten years ago. Now she's back in New York, and he isn't leaving until she comes with him. But the dark secret she once stumbled upon could unravel everything.

title: The Wrong Bride
author: Catharina Maura
synopsis: Raven has been secretly in love with Ares Windsor—billionaire media mogul—for years. When her sister abandons him at the altar, Raven is asked to take her place. Married at last to the man she's always loved, Raven is determined to win him over—but Ares has only ever had eyes for her sister. This is war.

title: Ruthless Creatures
author: J.T. Geissinger
synopsis: Five years after her fiancé vanished without a trace, Natalie is still haunted by unanswered questions—until a tall, dark, and dangerous stranger named Kage moves in next door. Drawn to him despite her instincts, she falls hard. Then she discovers he was sent to collect on her missing fiancé's debt. And that debt is her.

title: The Brutal Prince
author: Sophie Lark
synopsis: When Aida Gallo crashes a Griffin family party and accidentally sets the library on fire, it ignites all-out war between Chicago's rival Irish and Italian mafia families. The only solution: a shotgun marriage between the wild, headstrong Aida and Callum Griffin, the cold, brutal heir to the Irish mob—two people who would rather kill each other than say 'I do.'

title: Dr. Grant
author: Catharina Maura
synopsis: Dr. Noah Grant's dream job at a prestigious college came with three rules: no students, no patients, stay away from Harold Astor's granddaughter. He broke all three when Amara Astor—PhD student, patient, and the most off-limits woman alive—walked into his office. Getting involved risks everything he's worked for, but turning her away is impossible.

title: Empire of Hate
author: Rina Kent
synopsis: Nicole escaped her past—and the high school boy whose life she made hell—by flying across the ocean to start over. But her fresh start crumbles when she discovers her new boss is Daniel Sterling: rich, devastatingly attractive, and the very person she wronged. He's done waiting for his revenge.

title: Pretty Rings and Broken Things
author: Kat Singleton
synopsis: After a scandal threatens her family's reputation, Winnie is forced to marry Archer Moore—the cold, calculated billionaire heir to her family's rival company. The marriage is only for show, but Archer is very convincing. He demands they live together, kiss for appearances, and make it feel real. The problem is, he's starting to feel very real to her.

title: Liars Like Us
author: J.T. Geissinger
synopsis: Emery Eastwood is on the verge of losing her beloved family bookstore when mysterious billionaire Callum McCord walks in with a life-changing offer: a marriage of convenience. But Callum is obsessive and possessive, and Emery begins to wonder if she's the mark—or his entire world.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
synopsis: An exploration of the history of our species, from the emergence of Homo sapiens in Africa to the present day, focusing on how biology and history have defined us.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
synopsis: A memoir about a woman who leaves her survivalist family in the mountains of Idaho to pursue an education, eventually earning a PhD from Cambridge University.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
synopsis: A world-renowned psychologist explains the two systems that drive the way we think—one fast and intuitive, the other slow and deliberate.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
synopsis: A practical guide to breaking bad habits and building good ones by making small, incremental changes that lead to remarkable results.

title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
author: Rebecca Skloot
synopsis: The true story of a woman whose cancer cells were taken without her knowledge in 1951, becoming one of the most important tools in medicine.

title: Into Thin Air
author: Jon Krakauer
synopsis: A personal account of the 1996 Mount Everest disaster, detailing the grueling ascent and the tragic events that led to multiple deaths.

title: The Body Keeps the Score
author: Bessel van der Kolk
synopsis: A pioneering researcher explores how trauma reshapes both the body and the brain, and offers new paths to recovery.

title: Quiet: The Power of Introverts
author: Susan Cain
synopsis: An exploration of how modern Western culture misunderstands and undervalues the traits and capabilities of introverted people.

title: Becoming
author: Michelle Obama
synopsis: The former First Lady of the United States shares her journey from the South Side of Chicago to the White House.

title: The Devil in the White City
author: Erik Larson
synopsis: The true story of the 1893 World's Fair in Chicago and the cunning serial killer who used the fair to lure his victims.

title: When Breath Becomes Air
author: Paul Kalanithi
synopsis: A neurosurgeon's moving memoir about facing a terminal lung cancer diagnosis and what makes life worth living.

title: Blink: The Power of Thinking Without Thinking
author: Malcolm Gladwell
synopsis: An investigation into the mental processes that allow us to make split-second decisions and why they are often more accurate than long deliberation.

title: The Glass Castle
author: Jeannette Walls
synopsis: A memoir of resilience and redemption, and a revelatory look into a family at once deeply dysfunctional and uniquely vibrant.

title: A Short History of Nearly Everything
author: Bill Bryson
synopsis: An accessible and humorous journey through the history of science, from the Big Bang to the rise of civilization.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
synopsis: A psychiatrist’s memoir of surviving Nazi concentration camps and his development of logotherapy to find purpose in life.

title: Bad Blood
author: John Carreyrou
synopsis: The full inside story of the breathtaking rise and shocking collapse of Theranos, the multibillion-dollar biotech startup founded by Elizabeth Holmes.

title: The Power of Habit
author: Charles Duhigg
synopsis: Exploring the scientific discoveries that explain why habits exist and how they can be changed.

title: Born a Crime
author: Trevor Noah
synopsis: The story of a mischievous young boy who grows into a restless young man as he struggles to find himself in a world where he was never supposed to exist.

title: Hidden Figures
author: Margot Lee Shetterly
synopsis: The phenomenal true story of the African American female mathematicians at NASA whose calculations helped fuel some of America's greatest achievements in space.

title: Why We Sleep
author: Matthew Walker
synopsis: A neuroscientist explains the vital importance of sleep and how it affects every aspect of our physical and mental health.

title: The Gene: An Intimate History
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
synopsis: The story of the quest to decipher the master-code of instructions that makes us human and determines our heredity and health.

title: Killers of the Flower Moon
author: David Grann
synopsis: A true-life murder mystery about one of the most monstrous crimes in American history, involving the Osage Nation in Oklahoma.

title: Grit: The Power of Passion and Perseverance
author: Angela Duckworth
synopsis: A psychologist argues that the secret to outstanding achievement is not talent but a special blend of passion and persistence she calls 'grit.'

title: Kitchen Confidential
author: Anthony Bourdain
synopsis: An unfiltered look into the high-pressure, often chaotic world of professional kitchens, from a man who lived it.

title: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
synopsis: An ancient Chinese military treatise dating from the Late Spring and Autumn Period, attributed to the strategist Sun Tzu.

title: Outliers: The Story of Success
author: Malcolm Gladwell
synopsis: An examination of the factors that contribute to high levels of success, arguing that it is not just about individual merit.

title: Daring Greatly
author: Brené Brown
synopsis: A researcher-storyteller explores how vulnerability is not weakness, but rather our clearest path to courage, engagement, and meaningful connection.

title: The Tipping Point
author: Malcolm Gladwell
synopsis: Explaining how small ideas and trends can spread like viruses, reaching a point where they suddenly explode into massive changes.

title: Meditation for Fidgety Skeptics
author: Dan Harris
synopsis: A practical and humorous guide to meditation for people who think they can't do it or don't have the time.

title: The Year of Magical Thinking
author: Joan Didion
synopsis: A raw and powerful account of the year following the sudden death of the author's husband and the illness of her daughter.

title: Wild: From Lost to Found on the Pacific Crest Trail
author: Cheryl Strayed
synopsis: A memoir of a young woman who, after the death of her mother and the end of her marriage, decides to hike the PCT alone.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
synopsis: An argument for the value of deep, focused work in a world full of distractions, and how to cultivate this skill.

title: The Psychopath Test
author: Jon Ronson
synopsis: A journey through the madness industry, exploring how we define insanity and the people who walk the line.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
synopsis: A billionaire entrepreneur and investor shares his philosophy on how to build companies that create new things.

title: I'm Glad My Mom Died
author: Jennette McCurdy
synopsis: A raw and heartbreakingly funny memoir about the struggles of being a child star and dealing with a complicated mother-daughter relationship.

title: The Right Stuff
author: Tom Wolfe
synopsis: An account of the pilots engaged in U.S. postwar research with rocket-powered experimental aircraft and the first Project Mercury astronauts.

title: Just Mercy
author: Bryan Stevenson
synopsis: A powerful true story about the potential for mercy to redeem us, and a clarion call to fix our broken system of justice.

title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
author: Dale Carnegie
synopsis: One of the most famous self-help books of all time, offering time-tested advice on how to build better relationships and succeed in business.

title: The Omnivore's Dilemma
author: Michael Pollan
synopsis: An investigation into the modern food chain, following several different meals from their origin to the table.

title: Freakonomics
author: Steven D. Levitt
synopsis: Using economic theory to explore a wide range of diverse subjects, from the inner workings of a crack gang to why people cheat.

title: The Sixth Extinction
author: Elizabeth Kolbert
synopsis: An exploration of how human activities are causing the fastest and most widespread mass extinction in Earth's history.

title: Small Fry
author: Lisa Brennan-Jobs
synopsis: A coming-of-age memoir about a daughter's complicated relationship with her father, the visionary Steve Jobs.

title: Unbroken
author: Laura Hillenbrand
synopsis: The incredible true story of Louis Zamperini, an Olympic runner who survived a plane crash in the Pacific and became a prisoner of war during WWII.

title: The Emperor of All Maladies
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
synopsis: A comprehensive history of cancer, from its first documented appearances in antiquity to modern treatments and research.

title: Lab Girl
author: Hope Jahren
synopsis: A memoir by a geobiologist about her life in science, her love of plants, and her deep friendship with her lab partner.

title: The New Jim Crow
author: Michelle Alexander
synopsis: An examination of how the U.S. criminal justice system functions as a contemporary system of racial control.

title: Range: Why Generalists Triumph in a Specialized World
author: David Epstein
synopsis: An argument that the most successful people in many fields are those who start broad and dabble in many areas rather than specializing early.

title: Hidden Valley Road
author: Robert Kolker
synopsis: The heartrending story of a mid-century American family with twelve children, six of whom were diagnosed with schizophrenia.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
author: Mark Manson
synopsis: A counterintuitive approach to living a good life, arguing that we should focus on what truly matters and let go of the rest.

title: Breath: The New Science of a Lost Art
author: James Nestor
synopsis: An investigation into the history and science of breathing, and how changing the way we breathe can improve our health.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
synopsis: One of the most iconic science books of all time, Hawking explains complex concepts like the Big Bang, black holes, and the nature of time in a way that is accessible to non-scientists.

title: Make It Stick: The Science of Successful Learning
author: Peter C. Brown
synopsis: Drawing on recent discoveries in cognitive psychology and other disciplines, this book offers concrete techniques for becoming a more effective learner.

title: The Wealth of Nations
author: Adam Smith
synopsis: The foundational work of modern economics. Smith explores the mechanics of free markets, the division of labor, and the 'invisible hand' that drives productivity.

title: Mindset: The New Psychology of Success
author: Carol S. Dweck
synopsis: Dweck explains why it's not just our abilities and talent that bring us success, but whether we approach our goals with a fixed or growth mindset.

title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
author: Jared Diamond
synopsis: Diamond argues that the gaps in power and technology between human societies stem from environmental and geographical differences rather than biological ones.

title: The Republic
author: Plato
synopsis: A Socratic dialogue concerning justice, the order and character of the just city-state, and the just man. It is one of the world's most influential works of philosophy.

title: Basic Economics
author: Thomas Sowell
synopsis: A citizen's guide to the economy that explains economic principles without jargon or equations, using real-world examples from around the globe.

title: A Mind for Numbers
author: Barbara Oakley
synopsis: Oakley provides a guide on how to excel in math and science, even if you've struggled with them in the past, by understanding how the brain learns.

title: Lies My Teacher Told Me
author: James W. Loewen
synopsis: An examination of American history textbooks and the myths and misinformation they often promote, encouraging a more critical look at history.

title: Ultralearning
author: Scott H. Young
synopsis: Young shares strategies for mastering hard skills quickly, outsmarting the competition, and accelerating your career through self-directed learning.

title: Thinking Strategically
author: Avinash Dixit
synopsis: An introduction to game theory, showing how to use strategic thinking in business, politics, and everyday life to gain a competitive edge.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
synopsis: Dawkins introduces the gene-centered view of evolution, explaining how the drive for genetic survival explains complex biological behaviors.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
author: Robert T. Kiyosaki
synopsis: A personal finance classic that contrasts different philosophies on money, teaching the importance of financial literacy and building assets.

title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
author: Stephen R. Covey
synopsis: A step-by-step pathway for living with fairness, integrity, and human dignity—principles that give us the security to adapt to change.

title: Astrophysics for People in a Hurry
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson
synopsis: A thin, readable guide to the cosmos that covers the Big Bang, dark matter, and the search for life in the universe.

title: The Man Who Mistook His Wife for a Hat
author: Oliver Sacks
synopsis: Neurologist Oliver Sacks describes the case histories of patients lost in the bizarre world of neurological disorders.

title: Team of Rivals
author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
synopsis: A biographical study of Abraham Lincoln and his cabinet, exploring his political genius in uniting his former opponents for a common cause.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl
author: Anne Frank
synopsis: The personal diary of a young Jewish girl in hiding during the Nazi occupation of the Netherlands, providing an intimate look at the human spirit.

title: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings
author: Maya Angelou
synopsis: An autobiography illustrating how strength of character and a love of literature can help overcome trauma and racism.

title: The Smartest Kids in the World
author: Amanda Ripley
synopsis: Ripley follows three American students living abroad to investigate why some countries have much higher educational outcomes than others.

title: Being Logical: A Guide to Good Thinking
author: D.Q. McInerny
synopsis: A clear and concise guide to the principles of logic, helping readers develop the skills of coherent reasoning and effective communication.

title: The Genius of China
author: Robert K.G. Temple
synopsis: An exploration of 3,000 years of Chinese science and invention, revealing how many modern technologies had ancient Chinese origins.

title: How to Read a Book
author: Mortimer J. Adler
synopsis: The classic guide to intelligent reading, explaining different levels of reading and how to truly engage with a text.

title: Why Don't Students Like School?
author: Daniel T. Willingham
synopsis: A cognitive scientist explains how the brain works in a classroom setting and what it means for teaching and learning.

title: The 48 Laws of Power
author: Robert Greene
synopsis: Amoral, cunning, ruthless, and instructive, this book distills three thousand years of the history of power into 48 laws.

title: A Little History of Economics
author: Niall Kishtainy
synopsis: A beginner-friendly guide that traces the history of economic thought from ancient times to the modern global economy.

title: The Courage to Be Disliked
author: Ichiro Kishimi
synopsis: Based on Adlerian psychology, this book uses a dialogue between a philosopher and a young man to teach how to free yourself from the past.

title: Make It Stick
author: Peter C. Brown
synopsis: Highly recommended for students, this book explores why traditional study methods like re-reading don't work and what science says actually does.

title: Einstein: His Life and Universe
author: Walter Isaacson
synopsis: A biography exploring how Einstein's mind worked and how his rebellious nature led to his groundbreaking scientific discoveries.

title: Economics: The User's Guide
author: Ha-Joon Chang
synopsis: Chang explains how the global economy works and why many economic theories are not as 'scientific' as they claim to be.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
synopsis: Alicia Berenson is a famous painter who shoots her husband five times in the face and then never speaks another word. A criminal psychotherapist becomes obsessed with uncovering her motive.

title: The Way of Kings
author: Brandon Sanderson
synopsis: On a world of stone and storms, four characters lead a struggle for power and survival as ancient secrets of the Knights Radiant begin to resurface.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
synopsis: On the morning of their fifth wedding anniversary, Amy Dunne disappears. Under pressure from the police and a media frenzy, Nick’s portrait of a blissful marriage begins to crumble.

title: Circe
author: Madeline Miller
synopsis: The daughter of the sun god Helios is banished to a deserted island, where she hones her witchcraft and crosses paths with famous mythological figures like Hermes and Odysseus.

title: The Guest List
author: Lucy Foley
synopsis: A celebrity wedding on a remote island off the coast of Ireland turns deadly when a body is found. Everyone has a secret, and everyone has a motive.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
synopsis: Kvothe, a legendary figure, tells the story of his childhood in a traveling troupe, his years as a near-feral orphan, and his struggle to enter a school of magic.

title: Verity
author: Colleen Hoover
synopsis: A struggling writer accepts the job of a lifetime: finishing the book series of an injured world-famous author. While there, she finds a chilling unfinished autobiography.

title: Mexican Gothic
author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
synopsis: A glamorous socialite travels to a remote mansion in the mountains to save her cousin, only to discover a decaying family and a horrifying supernatural secret.

title: The House in the Cerulean Sea
author: TJ Klune
synopsis: A caseworker for magical youth is sent to an island orphanage to determine if six dangerous children could bring about the end of the world.

title: Dark Matter
author: Blake Crouch
synopsis: A physics professor is kidnapped and knocked unconscious, only to wake up in a world where his life is completely different. He must fight to find his way back to his true family.

title: None of This is True
author: Lisa Jewell
synopsis: A podcaster meets a woman on her birthday who claims they are 'birthday twins.' As they work on a podcast together, the woman’s life begins to invade the podcaster's in terrifying ways.

title: The 7 1/2 Deaths of Evelyn Hardcastle
author: Stuart Turton
synopsis: A man must solve a murder at a costume party, but every time the day ends, he wakes up in the body of a different guest.

title: Yellowface
author: R.F. Kuang
synopsis: When a successful Asian-American author dies, her 'friend' steals her manuscript and publishes it as her own, leading to a satirical spiral of lies and social media cancellation.

title: The Night Circus
author: Erin Morgenstern
synopsis: Two young magicians are pitted against each other in a magical competition within a traveling circus that only opens at night.

title: Normal People
author: Sally Rooney
synopsis: The story of the complex relationship between Marianne and Connell as they move from high school into their university years.

title: A Flicker in the Dark
author: Stacy Willingham
synopsis: The daughter of a serial killer thinks she’s finally moved on with her life, until local teenage girls begin to go missing in the exact same pattern as her father's crimes.

title: The Paris Apartment
author: Lucy Foley
synopsis: Jess goes to Paris to stay with her brother for a fresh start, only to find him missing and his neighbors suspicious and unfriendly.

title: The Last Thing He Told Me
author: Laura Dave
synopsis: Before vanishing, a man leaves a note for his wife: 'Protect her.' She must work with her stepdaughter to uncover why he disappeared.

title: Hidden Pictures
author: Jason Rekulak
synopsis: A nanny discovers that her five-year-old charge is drawing increasingly disturbing pictures that suggest a supernatural secret or a past murder.

title: Recursion
author: Blake Crouch
synopsis: A detective and a scientist team up to stop a phenomenon that allows people to rewrite their memories, threatening the stability of reality itself.

title: Daisy Jones & The Six
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
synopsis: An oral history of the rise and fall of a famous 1970s rock band and the magnetic lead singer who changed everything.

title: Then She Was Gone
author: Lisa Jewell
synopsis: Ten years after her daughter disappeared, a woman meets a man whose young daughter looks uncannily like her missing child.

title: The Locked Door
author: Freida McFadden
synopsis: A surgeon's father was a serial killer. Now, years later, her own patients are being murdered in the exact same way.

title: Home Before Dark
author: Riley Sager
synopsis: A woman returns to the house that was the subject of her father's famous ghost story to uncover the truth about what happened there twenty-five years ago.

title: A Little Life
author: Hanya Yanagihara
synopsis: Follows the lives of four friends in New York over decades, centering on Jude, a man haunted by an unspeakable childhood trauma.

title: Small Things Like These
author: Claire Keegan
synopsis: In a small Irish town in 1985, a coal merchant discovers a secret in the local convent that forces him to confront his conscience.

title: Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow
author: Gabrielle Zevin
synopsis: Two childhood friends become creative partners in the world of video game design, exploring the heights of success and the depths of personal loss.

title: Rock Paper Scissors
author: Alice Feeney
synopsis: A couple goes on a weekend getaway to a remote chapel in Scotland to save their marriage, but someone doesn't want them to leave.

title: Malibu Rising
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
synopsis: Four famous siblings throw an epic end-of-summer party in 1983. By morning, the house is in flames and family secrets are out.

title: The Maid
author: Nita Prose
synopsis: Molly Gray, a hotel maid who struggles with social cues, finds a wealthy guest dead in his bed and becomes the prime suspect.

title: Cloud Cuckoo Land
author: Anthony Doerr
synopsis: Spans five centuries and multiple perspectives, all linked by a single ancient Greek manuscript and the power of storytelling.

title: The Only One Left
author: Riley Sager
synopsis: A home health aide is hired to care for an old woman who was accused of murdering her family decades ago and has never spoken about it—until now.

title: Station Eleven
author: Emily St. John Mandel
synopsis: Moving between the days before a global pandemic and twenty years after, following a traveling troupe of actors and musicians.

title: The It Girl
author: Ruth Ware
synopsis: Ten years after her best friend was murdered at Oxford, a woman discovers that the man convicted of the crime might have been innocent.

title: Piranesi
author: Susanna Clarke
synopsis: Piranesi lives in a labyrinthine House of endless halls and statues. He explores his world with peace until another person brings chaos.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
synopsis: Ryland Grace wakes up on a spaceship with no memory of how he got there or how to save humanity from an extinction-level threat.

title: No Exit
author: Taylor Adams
synopsis: Stranded at a highway rest stop during a blizzard, a college student discovers a kidnapped child in the back of a van parked outside.

title: Bunny
author: Mona Awad
synopsis: A scholarship student in an elite MFA program is drawn into a cult-like clique of girls who call each other 'Bunny' and perform dark rituals.

title: Final Girl Support Group
author: Grady Hendrix
synopsis: A support group for women who survived real-life horror movie scenarios must fight back when someone starts hunting them down.

title: Demon Copperhead
author: Barbara Kingsolver
synopsis: A modern-day reimagining of David Copperfield set in the mountains of southern Appalachia, dealing with poverty and addiction.

title: The Chain
author: Adrian McKinty
synopsis: To save your kidnapped child, you must kidnap someone else's child. If you don't, the chain is broken and your child dies.

title: A Head Full of Ghosts
author: Paul Tremblay
synopsis: A family's struggle with their daughter's apparent possession is turned into a reality TV show, with devastating consequences.

title: My Sister, the Serial Killer
author: Oyinkan Braithwaite
synopsis: Korede is a nurse whose sister has a habit of killing her boyfriends. She helps clean up the mess until her sister eyes Korede's own crush.

title: The Whisper Man
author: Alex North
synopsis: A father and son move to a new town for a fresh start, only to find themselves targets of a serial killer who whispers at windows.

title: Annihilation
author: Jeff VanderMeer
synopsis: A team of four women enter Area X, a mysterious coastal zone where nature has begun to transform into something unrecognizable and deadly.

title: The Push
author: Ashley Audrain
synopsis: Blythe is determined to be the perfect mother, but she is convinced her daughter has a dark side that her husband refuses to see.

title: Upgrade
author: Blake Crouch
synopsis: Logan Ramsay's genome has been hacked. He begins to see the world differently, realizing he is being transformed into something beyond human.

title: The Southern Book Club's Guide to Slaying Vampires
author: Grady Hendrix
synopsis: A 1990s housewife joins a book club to escape her boring life, only to realize a charismatic newcomer in town is actually a predator.

title: Never Let Me Go
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
synopsis: Students at an elite boarding school slowly discover the unsettling truth about their purpose in a society that views them as less than human.

title: House of Leaves
author: Mark Z. Danielewski
synopsis: A family moves into a home that is larger on the inside than it is on the outside, leading to a descent into madness and architectural horror.

title: Serious Men
author: Manu Joseph
synopsis: Ayyan Mani, a middle-aged Dalit working as assistant to a brilliant Brahmin astronomer in Mumbai, grows increasingly furious at his lowly station in life and concocts an outrageous lie — that his 10-year-old son is a mathematical genius — a deception that spirals wildly out of control.

title: The Guide
author: R.K. Narayan
synopsis: Set in the fictional South Indian town of Malgudi, this Sahitya Akademi Award-winning novel traces Raju's transformation from a charming tour guide to a revered spiritual figure, exploring themes of identity, fate, and redemption.

title: Malgudi Days
author: R.K. Narayan
synopsis: A collection of 32 short stories, each offering a vivid slice of life in the fictional South Indian town of Malgudi — capturing ordinary people navigating love, ambition, superstition, and the quiet rhythms of everyday existence.

title: The Private Life of an Indian Prince
author: Mulk Raj Anand
synopsis: Set against the abolition of India's princely states after independence, the novel delves into the psychological unraveling of a maharaja who struggles to reconcile his feudal privileges with the demands of a newly democratic India.

title: Untouchable
author: Mulk Raj Anand
synopsis: Inspired by a real incident in Anand's family, the novel follows a single day in the life of Bakha, a young sweeper boy, exposing the brutal indignities of India's caste system and the urgent argument for its eradication.

title: Train to Pakistan
author: Khushwant Singh
synopsis: Set during the traumatic Partition of India in 1947, the novel focuses on a small border village where Hindus, Muslims and Sikhs have coexisted peacefully — until the tidal wave of communal violence arrives and tests every bond of humanity.

title: Godan
author: Munshi Premchand
synopsis: Originally written in Hindi and translated as 'The Gift of a Cow', Godan paints an unflinching portrait of a poor Indian farmer crushed by debt, exploitation, and the relentless socio-economic machinery of rural India.

title: The Room on the Roof
author: Ruskin Bond
synopsis: Rusty, an orphaned Anglo-Indian boy chafing under a strict guardian in Dehradun, breaks free to live among Indians — discovering friendship, freedom, and his own identity in this semi-autobiographical coming-of-age classic.

title: The Complete Adventures of Feluda
author: Satyajit Ray
synopsis: A beloved collection of 35 detective stories featuring master sleuth Pradosh C. Mitter (Feluda) and his companions, blending sharp reasoning with adventures spanning India's most vivid landscapes — translated into English by Gopa Majumdar.

title: Combat of Shadows
author: Manohar Malgonkar
synopsis: Ruby Miranda, an Anglo-Indian woman dreaming of marrying an Englishman and ascending the social ladder, is confronted instead with the harsh realities of racial discrimination in pre-independence India.

title: The Autobiography of an Unknown Indian
author: Nirad C. Chaudhuri
synopsis: A sweeping, introspective memoir that weaves personal growth with the political and cultural transformation of India — a serious and erudite reckoning with identity, colonialism, and the making of a nation.

title: The God of Small Things
author: Arundhati Roy
synopsis: The Booker Prize-winning novel follows fraternal twins Esthappen and Rahel, whose lives are shattered by the 'Love Laws' of society — exploring how small things affect people's behaviour, destinies, and capacity for love in Kerala's complex social landscape.

title: The White Tiger
author: Aravind Adiga
synopsis: Balram Halwai, a chauffeur from rural India, narrates his rise from poverty to entrepreneur in a darkly humorous first-person account that exposes the brutal class struggle and corruption at the heart of globalized India — winner of the Man Booker Prize.

title: The Inheritance of Loss
author: Kiran Desai
synopsis: Two stories intertwine: Biju, an illegal immigrant scraping by in New York, and Sai, an anglicised girl living with her retired judge grandfather in the Himalayas — both adrift in a world shaped by colonialism and its painful aftermath.

title: A Suitable Boy
author: Vikram Seth
synopsis: One of the longest single-volume novels in English, set in newly independent India, following four interconnected families over eighteen months as Mrs Rupa Mehra navigates tradition, politics, and love in her search for the right husband for her daughter Lata.

title: Sea of Poppies
author: Amitav Ghosh
synopsis: Aboard the Ibis, a former slave ship crossing the Indian Ocean on the eve of the Opium Wars, a diverse cast of characters — indentured laborers, convicts, an American sailor, and an opium-addicted raja — find their fates entangled in the tides of empire.

title: Narcopolis
author: Jeet Thayil
synopsis: A hypnotic descent into Bombay's opium dens of Shuklaji Street, following a handful of souls bound together by addiction — stripping the city of its glamour to reveal a shadowy, hallucinatory underworld of lives consumed by the pipe.

title: The Great Indian Novel
author: Shashi Tharoor
synopsis: A brilliant satirical tour de force that remaps the Mahabharata onto India's independence movement and post-colonial politics — transforming Vyasa, Bhishma, and Arjuna into thinly veiled versions of Gandhi, Nehru, and their political heirs.

title: Fasting, Feasting
author: Anita Desai
synopsis: Contrasting two worlds, the novel follows Uma, an unmarried daughter trapped by provincial Indian customs, and her brother Arun, studying in America, both prisoners in different ways of their family's consuming expectations.

title: The House of Blue Mangoes
author: David Davidar
synopsis: A gripping family chronicle spanning nearly half a century and three generations of the Dorai family in South India, vividly portraying a small community's struggle for identity against the backdrop of colonialism and rapid social change.

title: Vanity Bagh
author: Anees Salim
synopsis: Narrated by a boy from a minority community growing up in a neighbourhood of slow decay, this darkly funny novel is less about hope than about the grinding hopelessness, religious intolerance, and quiet desperation that divide communities and fuel elections.

title: Early India: From the Origins to AD 1300
author: Romila Thapar
synopsis: A vivid and authoritative exploration of India's ancient past — tracing the formation of a civilization across millennia through its politics, society, religion, trade, and culture, from prehistoric origins to the eve of the Delhi Sultanate.

title: History of Medieval India
author: Satish Chandra
synopsis: A comprehensive survey of India between the eighth and eighteenth centuries, covering the rise and fall of the Cholas, Rajputs, Turks, Vijayanagara, Mughals, and Marathas — a definitive academic account of a thousand years of political history.

title: From Plassey to Partition: A History of Modern India
author: Sekhar Bandyopadhyay
synopsis: An eminently readable account of two hundred years of political and socio-economic turbulence from 1757 to 1947, tracing India's freedom fighters, colonial struggles, and the painful Partition that gave birth to independent India and Pakistan.

title: India's Struggle for Independence
author: Bipan Chandra
synopsis: A meticulously researched and definitive account of India's freedom movement from 1857 to 1947, detailing Gandhi's civil disobedience campaigns, the rise of nationalism, and the many leaders who shaped the path to independence.

title: India's War: World War II and the Making of Modern South Asia
author: Srinath Raghavan
synopsis: Historian Srinath Raghavan argues that World War II was a pivotal turning point for South Asia — painting a compelling portrait of Indian soldiers fighting abroad and a home front transformed by the pressures that ultimately ended colonial rule.

title: Land of the Seven Rivers: A Brief History of India's Geography
author: Sanjeev Sanyal
synopsis: A fascinating journey through India's geography and how its rivers, mountains, and cities shaped thousands of years of history — a fresh, accessible lens through which to understand the subcontinent's civilizational evolution.

title: Himalayan Blunder
author: Brigadier John Dalvi
synopsis: A first-person account by a serving officer of the 1962 Sino-Indian War — a searing indictment of the military and political failures that led to India's most crushing military defeat, a book so explosive it was banned by the Indian government.

title: What Happened to Netaji?
author: Anuj Dhar
synopsis: An investigative account probing the enduring mystery of Subhas Chandra Bose's death — challenging the official air crash theory and examining alternative explanations, including the controversial Faizabad angle of the Bose mystery.

title: An Era of Darkness: The British Empire in India
author: Shashi Tharoor
synopsis: With sharp wit and meticulous research, Tharoor systematically dismantles the myth of benevolent British rule — laying bare how colonialism drained India's wealth, destroyed its industries, and engineered famines, while demolishing apologist arguments for empire.

title: The Complete Works of Swami Vivekananda
author: Swami Vivekananda
synopsis: An eight-volume compendium of all lectures, discourses, prose, poetry, and letters by the great spiritual reformer who brought Vedanta and Yoga to the Western world — an exhaustive record of a transformative philosophical and spiritual vision.

title: Mahabharata
author: C. Rajagopalachari (translator)
synopsis: An abridged English retelling of Vyasa's epic Mahabharata — capturing its vast sweep of heroic men and women, gods and demons, while distilling its timeless philosophy of ethics, duty, love, and the human condition into an accessible narrative.

title: Yajnaseni: The Story of Draupadi
author: Pratibha Ray
synopsis: A feminist retelling of the Mahabharata through Draupadi's own voice — exploring the inner life, desires, and dignity of one of India's most complex epic heroines, translated into English by Pradip Bhattacharya.

title: Ramayana Series
author: Ashok K. Banker
synopsis: An eight-book fantasy retelling of Valmiki's Ramayana — reimagining the ancient epic with the propulsive energy of modern fiction, following Rama's journey from the prince of Ayodhya to the slayer of Ravana across vivid mythological landscapes.

title: Myth = Mithya: A Handbook of Hindu Mythology
author: Devdutt Pattanaik
synopsis: Using his distinctive style of commentary, illustrations, and diagrams, Devdutt Pattanaik retells sacred Hindu stories and decodes ancient symbols and rituals — arguing that myth is not outdated superstition but a living, empowering language.

title: My Hanuman Chalisa
author: Devdutt Pattanaik
synopsis: Pattanaik demystifies Hinduism's most beloved hymn for the contemporary reader — unpacking the forty verses of the Hanuman Chalisa verse by verse, illuminated by his trademark illustrations and accessible commentary.

title: The Devil's Wind
author: Manohar Malgonkar
synopsis: Written as an imagined autobiography of Nana Saheb — the defiant leader of the 1857 War of Independence — Malgonkar humanizes a man demonized by the British, offering a sympathetic insider account of India's first great uprising.

title: Chanakya's Chant
author: Ashwin Sanghi
synopsis: A dual-narrative thriller interweaving ancient India's supreme strategist Chanakya with a modern political kingmaker — asking what would happen if Chanakya's ruthless genius were unleashed on contemporary Indian democracy.

title: Taj Mahal Trilogy
author: Indu Sundaresan
synopsis: A woman-centred Mughal saga told across three novels — The Twentieth Wife, The Feast of Roses, and Shadow Princess — following Mehrunnisa, Mumtaz Mahal, and Jahanara through the power, passion, and heartbreak of imperial India.

title: Harappa: Curse of the Blood River
author: Vineet Bajpai
synopsis: A historical thriller weaving 3,700 years of ancient and modern-day characters into a nail-biting conspiracy — the first of a four-book saga blending violence, deceit, gods, demons, and the lost civilization of the Indus Valley.

title: Empire
author: Devi Yesodharan
synopsis: A Greek woman warrior, captured and enslaved by the forces of Rajendra Chola, becomes an archer and fighter in the vast empire he ruled from 1014–44 — a tale of survival, identity, and power across cultures.

title: Zelaldinus: A Masque
author: Irwin Allan Sealy
synopsis: A playful and erudite fiction in which the great Mughal Emperor Akbar and a present-day tourist named Irv rediscover together the ghost city of Fatehpur Sikri — a meditation on history, art, power, and the passage of time.

title: The Shiva Trilogy
author: Amish Tripathi
synopsis: Built on the radical premise that all gods were once human, this three-book saga reimagines the legend of Shiva — following a Tibetan tribal chief as he rises to become India's greatest protector across The Immortals of Meluha, The Secret of the Nagas, and The Oath of the Vayuputras.

title: Asura: Tale of the Vanquished
author: Anand Neelakantan
synopsis: A radical retelling of the Ramayana from Ravana's perspective — and that of Bhadra, a common Asura foot soldier — giving voice to the vanquished and forcing readers to question the victor's version of one of India's most beloved epics.

title: The Palace of Illusions
author: Chitra Banerjee Divakaruni
synopsis: The Mahabharata retold through Draupadi's eyes — from her miraculous birth in fire to her role as wife to five Pandava brothers — a rich, feminist reimagining of the epic that places a woman's perspective at its centre.

title: The Aryavarta Chronicles
author: Krishna Udayasankar
synopsis: A reinvention of the Mahabharata seen through the eyes of Govinda Shauri — weaving familiar Kaurava and Pandava characters into a fresh, morally complex narrative that challenges established ideas of heroes and villains.

title: Thundergod: The Ascendance of Indra
author: Rajiv G. Menon
synopsis: Born of a prophetic union between the Earth Goddess and the chief of the Devas, Indra's story is rendered as a compelling saga blended from history, legend, and myth — tracing the ascendance of the greatest of the Vedic gods.

title: Karna's Wife: The Outcast's Queen
author: Kavita Kane
synopsis: The Mahabharata's great tragic hero Karna is seen through the devoted eyes of his wife Uruvi — a princess who chooses to marry a man rejected by society, bringing a unique and moving perspective to the unsung hero's story.

title: Saraswati's Intelligence
author: Vamsee Juluri
synopsis: The first book in the Kishkindha Chronicles reimagines India's ancient prehistory from a startling new perspective — asking what it means to be human and animal through a world where gods, beasts, and civilizations are still taking shape.

title: Raavan: Enemy of Aryavarta
author: Amish Tripathi
synopsis: The third book in the Ram Chandra series gives the demon king Ravana his own origin story — a rich blend of action, mythology, and fantasy that paints the legendary antagonist as a man of fierce ambition and complex morality.

title: Keepers of the Kalachakra
author: Ashwin Sanghi
synopsis: A gripping thriller anchored in the Vajrayana Buddhist concept of the Kalachakra — blending mystical traditions, scientific theories, historical incidents, and conspiracy into a breathless race to prevent a catastrophic event.

title: The One You Cannot Have
author: Preeti Shenoy
synopsis: A nuanced exploration of love, loss, and depression — following characters whose lives are forever altered by loves they could not keep, and asking whether a more mature handling of heartbreak might have saved them.

title: I Too Had a Love Story
author: Ravinder Singh
synopsis: Ravin and Khushi meet on a matrimonial site and fall tenderly in love — until fate intervenes with a devastating blow. A heartfelt, semi-autobiographical tale that reminds us not every love story is destined for a happy ending.

title: This is Not Your Story
author: Savi Sharma
synopsis: Four young people — Shaurya, Miraya, Anubhav, and Kasturi — each wrestling with personal struggles, find that daring to dream fearlessly and giving love a second chance can transform even the most broken lives.

title: The Girl of My Dreams
author: Durjoy Datta
synopsis: Daman wakes from a coma after a car accident with no memory except a name — Shreyasi — and a hazy vision of the girl who haunts his dreams and may hold the key to the life he cannot remember.

title: It's All in the Planets
author: Preeti Shenoy
synopsis: Four people, two love stories, and an astrologer who connects them all — a fresh, warm romantic novel about how the lives of very different people collide and change each other in ways that transcend age and geography.

title: Sorry, You're Not My Type
author: Sudeep Nagarkar
synopsis: The story of VAYU, a Delhi college music band, whose members discover that when passion and purpose eclipse fear, love and success become possible — a young, spirited novel about chasing dreams and finding connection.

title: Those Pricey Thakur Girls
author: Anuja Chauhan
synopsis: Set in 1988 Delhi, this breezy, witty novel follows the five Thakur daughters — named alphabetically — as they navigate ambition, love, and family expectations, with youngest Debjani falling for a crusading journalist.

title: 2 States: The Story of My Marriage
author: Chetan Bhagat
synopsis: Partly autobiographical, this comic novel follows a Punjabi boy and a Tamil girl madly in love — but first they must win over two wildly different families in a cross-cultural battle that tests every bond of patience and affection.

title: A Half-Baked Love Story
author: Anurag Garg
synopsis: Aarav confesses every detail of his first love — for Anamika — to his friends on a New Year's night, in a candid, youthful narrative about the messiness, joy, and heartbreak of falling in love for the first time.

title: Just Married, Please Excuse
author: Yashodhara Lal
synopsis: A quick-tempered city girl and a laid-back desi boy attempt to navigate the comedy and chaos of newlywed life — helped (and hindered) by eccentric staff, meddling families, and their nutty friend Vivi in this warmly funny debut.

title: Spy in the Amber
author: Manohar Malgonkar
synopsis: Set during peak Sino-Indian tensions, a remote Himalayan monastery sitting on a treasure worth millions becomes the target of a Chinese plot — a classic espionage thriller pitting India's sovereignty against a ruthless adversary.

title: Sacred Games
author: Vikram Chandra
synopsis: A monumental crime novel drawing Inspector Sartaj Singh into the vast criminal underworld of Ganesh Gaitonde, Mumbai's most wanted gangster — a sweeping story of friendship, betrayal, violence, and a city's dark soul.

title: Adventures of Byomkesh Bakshi
author: Sharadindu Bandyopadhyay
synopsis: The collected adventures of Byomkesh Bakshi, Bengali fiction's most beloved detective — a cerebral 'Seeker of Truth' who made his debut in the 1930s and has delighted generations of readers with his ingenious, witty solutions.

title: If God Was a Banker
author: Ravi Subramanian
synopsis: Two management graduates race for the top of a bank — Sundeep driven by selfish ambition and unscrupulous shortcuts, Swami guided by ethics and integrity — in a corporate thriller about how we define and compromise success.

title: Murder in a Minute
author: Shouvik Bhattacharya
synopsis: When a young woman is found dead and everyone points to her college sweetheart, her step-brothers Rishabh and Arya refuse to accept the obvious — embarking on a dangerous investigation riddled with planted evidence and conspiracy.

title: Marry Me, Stranger
author: Novoneel Chakraborty
synopsis: Rivanah Bannerjee, a young woman living alone in Mumbai, realizes someone is watching her every move — and their control over her life is growing. A dangerously seductive psychological thriller about obsession and identity.

title: Patang
author: Bhaskar Chattopadhyay
synopsis: A man is found murdered, his body posed like a kite atop a cell tower — as more corpses appear in bizarre configurations, maverick investigator Chandrakant Rathod races to match wits with a sadistic and methodical serial killer.

title: The Murder of Sonia Raikkonen
author: Salil Desai
synopsis: The mutilated corpse of a young Finnish tourist surfaces in a Pune garden — what looks like a brutal rape and murder draws Senior Inspector Saralkar and PSI Motkar into a far deeper and more twisted investigation.

title: Dead in a Mumbai Minute
author: Madhumita Bhattacharya
synopsis: Detective Reema Ray is handed the career-defining case of the Bollywood world's most glamorous murder — set on a private island, with every houseguest (including the host herself) a potential suspect.

title: A Cut Like Wound
author: Anita Nair
synopsis: On the first night of Ramadan, a young male prostitute is burnt alive — as the body count rises, Inspector Borei Gowda recognizes a pattern in the killings no one else sees, leading him into a dark investigation into Bangalore's hidden worlds.

title: Professor Shonku
author: Satyajit Ray
synopsis: Inventor and scientist Professor Trilokeshwar Shonku embarks on a series of fantastical adventures across the globe — a beloved Bengali sci-fi classic translated into English, combining wit, imagination, and gentle wonder.

title: GameWorld Trilogy
author: Samit Basu
synopsis: Three books — The Simoqin Prophecies, The Manticore's Secret, and The Unwaba Revelations — tell the story of the reawakening of the terrible rakshas Danh-Gem and the unlikely hero fated to face him, in India's landmark contribution to high fantasy.

title: The Rise of Sivagami
author: Anand Neelakantan
synopsis: A prequel to the blockbuster Baahubali films, tracing the origins of the fierce and brilliant Sivagami — and how a young woman of conviction forged herself into the iron queen who would shape the fate of Mahishmati.

title: The Devourers
author: Indra Das
synopsis: Spanning the Mughal Empire of the seventeenth century and modern Kolkata, this multi-layered novel features shape-shifting werewolves and explores questions of love, cannibalism, and what it means to be human — one of Indian fantasy's most acclaimed novels.

title: Dark Things
author: Sukanya Venkatraghavan
synopsis: A strange fairytale woven from Indian folklore, combining fantasy with shadow and enchantment — following forces of evil through a world of intrigue and secrets, where ancient malevolence meets the courage of those who resist it.

title: Cult of Chaos
author: Shweta Taneja
synopsis: In a world where Tantriks have emerged from the shadows into their own council and justice system, detective Anantya Tantrik must stop a catastrophic clash between White and Red Tantriks while a Black tantric brews a lethal power.

title: Savage Blue
author: Vikram Balagopal
synopsis: Shyam has never forgotten the night Akila disappeared when they were children — so when she reappears twenty years later with stories of surreal parallel worlds and strange creatures, he must decide how much of her reality he can accept.

title: Nine: Curse of the Kalingan
author: Shobha Nihalani
synopsis: For millennia, the wisdom of the NINE has protected humanity from itself — but a vengeful ancient spirit returns, possessing a young man and marshalling black yogis to destroy the order, in this Indian fantasy steeped in mysticism.

title: Skyfire
author: Aroon Raman
synopsis: India is hit by catastrophic weather disturbances and epidemics while children vanish from Delhi's slums — journalist Chandrasekhar, historian Meenakshi Pirzada, and intelligence operative Syed Ali Hassan race to uncover the terrifying truth behind it all.

title: Alice in Deadland Trilogy
author: Mainak Dhar
synopsis: Fifteen years after civilization collapsed, fifteen-year-old Alice has grown up fighting zombified Biters in the wastelands — until one fateful chase leads her into an underground world that turns everything she knows upside down.

title: Dork Trilogy
author: Sidin Vadukut
synopsis: The adventures and spectacular misadventures of Robin 'Einstein' Varghese — academically gifted (41st in his class), naïvely ambitious, and gloriously clueless as he navigates corporate life across three riotously funny books.

title: Mrs Funnybones
author: Twinkle Khanna
synopsis: Full of wit and sharp observations, this collection captures the daily life of the modern Indian woman — managing dinner, a career, a Mummyji, her weight, and the state of the country with equal doses of exasperation and self-deprecating humor.

title: Gone with the Vindaloo
author: Vikram Nair
synopsis: Two parallel stories run side by side: the cook Pakwaan striving to master his father's culinary legacy, and the tale of his legendary grandfather Kalaam, whose Vindaloo became the stuff of family myth — a charming novel about food, memory, and inheritance.

title: The Competent Authority
author: Shovon Chowdhury
synopsis: Set in a 2030s India reduced to rubble by a Chinese nuclear strike, this satirical masterpiece skewers the government, bureaucracy, police, army, and spiritual gurus with savage wit in an absurdist story of power, corruption, and survival.

title: Trust Me
author: Rajashree
synopsis: Set against the glittering backdrop of Bollywood, this comic story follows Parvati — freshly dumped and swearing off men — as she rediscovers herself through friendship, heartbreak, and the chaotic charm of the film industry.

title: Piece of Cake
author: Swati Kaushal
synopsis: Minal wants it all — career success, a fabulous lifestyle, and the perfect man who brings diamonds and laughs at her jokes. Given her spectacular track record of embarrassing herself, it won't exactly be a piece of cake.

title: The Zoya Factor
author: Anuja Chauhan
synopsis: Cricket-hating ad executive Zoya becomes the Indian national team's unlikely mascot — a wildly funny first-person romp about superstition, sport, celebrity, and a reluctant lucky charm who finds herself at the centre of World Cup hysteria.

title: Almost Single
author: Advaita Kala
synopsis: Aisha Bhatia is a smart, irreverent young woman navigating the competing demands of personal independence and matrimonial expectation in a culture-bound world — a heartfelt and wickedly funny cross-cultural debut novel.

title: Confessions of a Listmaniac
author: Meenakshi Reddy Madhavan
synopsis: Seventeen-year-old Layla decides to keep a retro notebook journal instead of a blog — and her entries bloom into quirky, hilarious lists recording her observations on teenage life, love, and the utterly baffling adult world around her.

title: I Quit! Now What?
author: Zarreen Khan
synopsis: After seven underpaid, undervalued years, marketing professional Nimisha quits her job in a blaze of justified fury — and then must face the more terrifying question of what comes next in this relatable, spirited novel about reinvention.

title: Encounters of a Fat Bride
author: Samah Visaria
synopsis: At 93 kilos, twenty-five-year-old Madhurima Pandey is deemed the wrong weight for marriage by her family — even if she's the ideal age. A funny, tender novel about body image, matrimonial absurdity, and finding confidence on your own terms.

title: The Story of My Experiments with Truth
author: Mahatma Gandhi
synopsis: Gandhi's autobiography covers his childhood through to 1921, tracing his transformation from a shy, privileged young man into the moral and political leader who would challenge British rule — a candid account of spiritual discipline, failures, and conviction.

title: Playing It My Way
author: Sachin Tendulkar
synopsis: The autobiography of India's cricketing god — covering Sachin Tendulkar's childhood, 24-year international career, and the private aspects of his extraordinary life that have never before been shared publicly.

title: My Days: A Memoir
author: R.K. Narayan
synopsis: In his characteristic warm, humorous style, Narayan recounts his life story — from childhood in his grandmother's Madras garden with a ferocious pet peacock, to his discovery of the fictional Malgudi and a lifetime of beloved writing.

title: Lone Fox Dancing: My Autobiography
author: Ruskin Bond
synopsis: Ruskin Bond traces the roots of his writing life — from an idyllic childhood by the Arabian Sea and wartime Delhi, through his years in the hills that shaped his sensibility, revealing the lived experiences behind some of India's most treasured literary works.

title: Wings of Fire: An Autobiography
author: A.P.J. Abdul Kalam
synopsis: The inspiring autobiography of India's 'Missile Man' — tracing Kalam's humble origins, his hardships, and the perseverance that led him to spearhead India's space, nuclear, and missile programs and eventually the Presidency.

title: Autobiography of a Yogi
author: Paramahansa Yogananda
synopsis: A spiritual classic narrating Yogananda's journey from boyhood curiosity about India's sages to his meetings with Gandhi, Tagore, and Therese Neumann — a captivating portrait of a mystic who brought yoga and Vedanta to the Western world.

title: The Accidental Prime Minister
author: Sanjaya Baru
synopsis: Prime Minister Manmohan Singh's former media advisor reveals the inner workings of the PMO — alleging that Singh exercised limited control over his cabinet while real power remained firmly in the hands of Congress president Sonia Gandhi.

title: Our Moon Has Blood Clots
author: Rahul Pandita
synopsis: The harrowing personal account of the ethnic cleansing of Kashmir's Pandit community — a story largely silenced in public discourse — told by a survivor who recounts the violence, loss, and exile that destroyed a centuries-old way of life.

title: Rajinikanth: The Definitive Biography
author: Naman Ramachandran
synopsis: A meticulous chronicle of Rajinikanth's extraordinary cinematic journey — from his debut in Apoorva Raagangal in 1975 through decades of mega-hits — offering rare insights into the personal life behind the superstar myth.

title: Veerappan: Chasing the Brigand
author: K. Vijay Kumar
synopsis: The most decorated officer who hunted Veerappan for years delivers a lucid, incisive account of the rise and fall of India's most feared forest brigand — the man behind the legendary moustache, the massacres, and the elusive escapes.

title: The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari
author: Robin Sharma
synopsis: A fable about Julian Mantle, a high-flying lawyer who suffers a heart attack in court and journeys to the Himalayan monasteries — returning with ancient wisdom on purpose, passion, and peace that transforms his own life and those around him.

title: Who Will Cry When You Die?
author: Robin Sharma
