synopsis: Robin Sharma offers 101 practical, compassionate strategies for life's most complex challenges — from beating stress and worry to building a legacy worth leaving — in a short, powerful guide to a life lived with greater meaning.

title: You Can Win
author: Shiv Khera
synopsis: Drawing on ancient wisdom and modern philosophy, Khera argues that success is deliberate and learnable — offering practical tools to handle daily challenges, reverse negative situations, and build the attitude of a winner.

title: Inner Engineering: A Yogi's Guide to Joy
author: Sadhguru
synopsis: Sadhguru presents the classical science of yoga as a path to absolute well-being — a guide to becoming the architect of your own inner world, turning you from a product of your circumstances into the master of your own joy.

title: Chanakya's 7 Secrets of Leadership
author: Radhakrishnan Pillai
synopsis: Drawing from the teachings of the 4th-century BC strategist Chanakya, Pillai constructs a model of leadership built on timeless principles of strategy, ethics, and power — as relevant to today's corporate world as to ancient kingdoms.

title: 13 Steps to Bloody Good Luck
author: Ashwin Sanghi
synopsis: Sanghi investigates that most coveted of commodities — luck — through entertaining anecdotes, personal experience, and homespun wisdom, arguing that the conditions for luck can be cultivated and that fortune favours the prepared.

title: The Habit of Winning
author: Prakash Iyer
synopsis: A collection of stories about leadership, teamwork, self-belief, and perseverance — drawn from cola wars, cricket, Olympic heroes, and ordinary lives — designed to change the way readers think, work, and live.

title: The Wellness Sense
author: Om Swami
synopsis: Drawing on the esoteric wisdom of ancient traditions, Om Swami offers a practical guide to better self-care and a healthier life in the modern world — where material comfort is abundant but genuine rest, stillness, and nourishment remain elusive.

title: Around India in 80 Trains
author: Monisha Rajesh
synopsis: Inspired by Jules Verne, Monisha Rajesh embarks on a 40,000 km rail odyssey across India — a distance equal to the circumference of the Earth — rediscovering a country that had grown unfamiliar, through its trains, stations, and the vivid lives aboard them.

title: When the Road Beckons
author: Ravi Manoram
synopsis: Escaping the inescapable grind of modern life, the protagonist sets off on a 4,000 km solo motorbike journey across Ladakh — an inspiring odyssey of self-discovery along one of the world's most breathtaking landscapes.

title: Following Fish: Travels Around the Indian Coast
author: Samanth Subramanian
synopsis: Journeying along India's vast and diverse coastline, Subramanian follows fish into the heart of many worlds — food, culture, commerce, sport, history, and society — reporting a kaleidoscope of extraordinary stories from the edges of the peninsula.

title: Hot Tea Across India
author: Rishad Saam Mehta
synopsis: A chai-fuelled journey from Manali to Munnar and from the Rann of Kutch to Khajuraho — where every cup of tea on every highway brings a new encounter and reveals the endlessly fascinating adventure of travelling through India.

title: Battlefields & Paradise
author: Sabir Hussain
synopsis: A solo motorcycle travelogue from Delhi to Turtuk — India's northernmost point on the Line of Control — where the author rides an ordinary bike through extraordinary landscapes and finds extraordinary humanity in ordinary Kashmiris.

title: Butter Chicken in Ludhiana: Travels in Small Town India
author: Pankaj Mishra
synopsis: Mishra captures an India shedding its sleepy socialist past in a rush toward kitschy, clamorous modernity — travelling through small towns to paint a vivid, often uncomfortable portrait of a people hurtling toward their tryst with the contemporary world.

title: All Roads Lead to Ganga
author: Ruskin Bond
synopsis: Ruskin Bond captures the breathtaking spiritual and natural splendour of the Ganges — describing with nostalgia and affection the places and people encountered along India's most sacred river over four decades of devoted living and observing.

title: The Heat and Dust Project
author: Devapriya Roy
synopsis: Two writers abandon their desks for backpacks and set off to cross India on just five hundred rupees a day for food and board — a transformational journey of misadventure, intimacy, and discovery across the subcontinent.

title: If It's Monday It Must Be Madurai
author: Srinath Perur
synopsis: An entertaining travelogue following ten conducted group tours — from camel rides in the Thar to ancient pilgrimages, Kabir music trails, and sex tourism in Tashkent — a warm, witty exploration of how and why Indians travel.

title: The Land of Flying Lamas
author: Gaurav Punj
synopsis: A journey into the 'other Himalaya' beyond the tourist trail — where flowers bloom in rolling meadows, glaciers are within reach, and high mountain passes open onto even more magical lands beyond, in this evocative travelogue.

title: Think and Grow Rich
author: Napoleon Hill
synopsis: Drawing on two decades of research into the habits of over 500 of America's most successful individuals, Hill distills a philosophy of achievement into 13 timeless principles — arguing that desire, belief, and positive thinking are the true foundations of wealth and success.

title: The Magic of Thinking Big
author: David J. Schwartz
synopsis: Schwartz argues that success has little to do with innate intelligence or luck — and everything to do with the size of your thinking. A practical, uplifting program for earning more, leading better, and living a bigger, happier life by reshaping thought habits.

title: The Power of Positive Thinking
author: Norman Vincent Peale
synopsis: Peale's landmark guide to harnessing the power of faith and optimism — offering practical techniques to overcome self-doubt, break the habit of worrying, and build an unshakeable confidence that transforms everyday life.

title: The 48 Laws of Power
author: Robert Greene
synopsis: Drawing on the lives of history's most cunning strategists — from Machiavelli to Louis XIV — Greene distills 3,000 years of power dynamics into 48 ruthlessly practical laws, a handbook for those who wish to understand and master the game of influence.

title: You Are a Badass
author: Jen Sincero
synopsis: Part coach, part comedian, Sincero delivers a funny, no-nonsense guide to identifying self-sabotaging beliefs, embracing your inner greatness, and making the money, career, and life you actually want — without apology.

title: The Daily Stoic
author: Ryan Holiday
synopsis: 365 meditations drawn from the writings of Marcus Aurelius, Epictetus, and Seneca — a practical guide to applying ancient Stoic wisdom to modern daily life, one page at a time.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
synopsis: Newport argues that the ability to focus without distraction on cognitively demanding tasks is the superpower of the 21st century — offering rules and strategies for cultivating this rare skill and producing remarkable output in a world of constant distraction.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
synopsis: Through 19 short stories, Housel explores the strange ways people think about money — arguing that financial success has more to do with behavior, patience, and humility than with intelligence or financial knowledge.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
synopsis: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl recounts his survival in Nazi concentration camps and the logotherapy he developed in their shadow — arguing that meaning, not pleasure or power, is the deepest human motivation and the key to enduring any suffering.

title: Feel the Fear and Do It Anyway
author: Susan Jeffers
synopsis: Jeffers offers the counterintuitive insight that fear never disappears — and that the path to confidence and freedom lies not in eliminating it but in acting in spite of it, with practical tools to push past paralysis into a fuller life.

title: The 5 Love Languages
author: Gary Chapman
synopsis: Chapman identifies five distinct ways people give and receive love — Words of Affirmation, Acts of Service, Receiving Gifts, Quality Time, and Physical Touch — and shows how understanding your own and your partner's language can transform relationships.

title: Big Magic: Creative Living Beyond Fear
author: Elizabeth Gilbert
synopsis: Gilbert makes a passionate, whimsical case for a creative life — arguing that ideas are living things seeking human collaborators, and that facing fear with curiosity rather than paralysis is the secret to a life of inspiration and wonder.

title: Feeling Good: The New Mood Therapy
author: David D. Burns
synopsis: The clinical bible of cognitive behavioral therapy made accessible — Burns shows readers how to identify and break the distorted thinking patterns that fuel depression, anxiety, and low self-esteem, offering a drug-free path to lasting emotional wellbeing.

title: The Push
author: Ashley Audrain
synopsis: Blythe Connor becomes a mother consumed by the terrifying suspicion that something is deeply wrong with her daughter — a searing, page-turning novel about maternal ambivalence, generational trauma, and the limits of unconditional love.

title: The Inmate
author: Freida McFadden
synopsis: A nurse starts working at a correctional facility — only to discover that an inmate she knows from her past is imprisoned there. A propulsive psychological thriller filled with secrets, reversals, and a past that refuses to stay buried.

title: Before I Go to Sleep
author: S.J. Watson
synopsis: Christine wakes up every morning with no memory of her past — a result of amnesia that resets each night. As she pieces her history together through a secret journal, she begins to suspect that the people closest to her are lying.

title: The Wife Between Us
author: Greer Hendricks & Sarah Pekkanen
synopsis: What looks like a story about a jealous ex-wife obsessing over her husband's new fiancée turns out to be something far more sinister — a meticulously plotted thriller full of shocking reversals that challenge every assumption about who is the victim.

title: Behind Her Eyes
author: Sarah Pinborough
synopsis: A single mother becomes entangled in the lives of her married boss and his wife — developing friendships with both while keeping each a secret from the other, until a shocking ending rewrites everything the reader thought they knew.

title: A Flicker in the Dark
author: Stacy Willingham
synopsis: Chloe Davis, a psychologist whose father is imprisoned for a series of murders, starts to suspect history is repeating itself when girls in her Louisiana town begin to disappear — a haunting debut thriller about inherited darkness and buried truth.

title: The Guest List
author: Lucy Foley
synopsis: A glamorous island wedding in the Atlantic storms descends into murder — as multiple narrators reveal their secrets and grievances, everyone at the guest list has a motive and nobody can leave.

title: Lock Every Door
author: Riley Sager
synopsis: Desperate for money, Jules moves into a coveted apartment-sitting position in a grand and storied New York building — only to begin suspecting that the other residents and the building itself are hiding something deeply wrong.

title: My Darling Girl
author: Jennifer McMahon
synopsis: When Alison agrees to care for her estranged dying mother, strange and terrifying things begin to happen — blurring the line between the supernatural and the psychological in this gothic, emotionally charged horror thriller.

title: Fractured
author: Karin Slaughter
synopsis: A seemingly routine case of self-defence in a suburban Atlanta home unravels into something far darker, forcing detective Will Trent and medical examiner Sara Linton into a web of violence, buried secrets, and predators hiding in plain sight.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
synopsis: Alicia Berenson is a famous painter who shoots her husband five times in the face and then never speaks another word. A criminal psychotherapist becomes obsessed with uncovering her motive.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
synopsis: On the morning of their fifth wedding anniversary, Amy Dunne vanishes — and as the media frenzy builds around her husband Nick, alternating narrators reveal a marriage built on lies, control, and stunning mutual deception.

title: The Housemaid
author: Freida McFadden
synopsis: When Millie takes a housemaid job with the seemingly perfect Winchester family, she senses something is deeply wrong — a viral, addictive thriller full of dark secrets and a shocking twist about who is really trapped and who holds the power.

title: Behind Closed Doors
author: B.A. Paris
synopsis: Jack and Grace Angel appear to have the perfect marriage — but as their neighbors grow closer, they discover that behind closed doors lies a carefully constructed nightmare of coercion and terror that Grace is desperate to escape.

title: Verity
author: Colleen Hoover
synopsis: Struggling writer Lowen Ashby discovers a manuscript hidden in bestselling author Verity Crawford's home — an autobiography that reveals disturbing truths about Verity's marriage and her children's deaths, forcing Lowen into an impossible moral dilemma.

title: The Girl on the Train
author: Paula Hawkins
synopsis: Rachel, an alcoholic divorcée, becomes obsessed with a couple she watches from her commuter train each day — until one morning the woman disappears, and Rachel finds herself entangled in a missing persons investigation she may have witnessed.

title: The Woman in the Window
author: A.J. Finn
synopsis: Agoraphobic psychologist Anna Fox spends her days observing her neighbours from her Manhattan home — until she witnesses something she was never meant to see, and no one believes her account of the crime.

title: Rock Paper Scissors
author: Alice Feeney
synopsis: A couple whose marriage is quietly crumbling retreats to a remote Scottish chapel for a weekend getaway — only to find that someone knows their darkest secrets, in a twisty thriller where identity and motive are never what they seem.

title: None of This Is True
author: Lisa Jewell
synopsis: Podcaster Alix Summer befriends the unremarkable Josie Fair — a woman who shares her birthday — and agrees to feature her on her podcast, not realising that Josie's story is far more dangerous and consuming than it appears.

title: Sharp Objects
author: Gillian Flynn
synopsis: Journalist Camille Preaker returns to her small Missouri hometown to cover the murders of two young girls — and is forced to confront her own traumatic past, her disturbed mother, and the ways small towns conceal their deepest wounds.

title: Garlic and Sapphires: The Secret Life of a Critic in Disguise
author: Ruth Reichl
synopsis: Ruth Reichl, upon becoming the New York Times' top restaurant critic, discovers restaurants have her picture posted everywhere. To review them honestly, she adopts a series of elaborate disguises and personas — from frumpy schoolteachers to flamboyant redheads — and discovers how dramatically a restaurant's treatment changes based on who you appear to be.

reading_insights.reading_value: Wit, empathy, and social commentary through food

title: Delicious!
author: Ruth Reichl
synopsis: Billie Breslin moves from California to New York to work at Delicious!, the city's most iconic food magazine. When the magazine shuts down, she stays to man the reader hotline — and stumbles upon a hidden room containing wartime letters written by a young girl to the legendary chef James Beard, setting Billie on a journey of self-discovery, history, and love.

reading_insights.reading_value: Healing through food, history, and human connection

title: The Gastronomical Me
author: M.F.K. Fisher
synopsis: A seminal classic of food writing, this memoir traces M.F.K. Fisher's life through the meals she ate and the people she shared them with — from childhood in California to Dijon in the 1930s, across cruise ships and Swiss farms, through love, loss, and the tensions of pre-war Europe. Food here is metaphor: for desire, survival, grief, and joy.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Literary appreciation and philosophical reflection on appetite
reading_insights.reading_value: Prose mastery, cultural history, and the meaning of hunger

title: An Everlasting Meal: Cooking with Economy and Grace
author: Tamar Adler
synopsis: A meditation on the philosophy and practice of cooking, this essay collection teaches readers to cook instinctively and frugally — using leftovers, bones, peels, and humble ingredients to create satisfying meals. Written in the spirit of M.F.K. Fisher, it weaves philosophy with practical instruction and argues that the best meals grow from the ends of previous ones.

reading_insights.reading_value: Practical wisdom, mindfulness, and beauty in simplicity

title: The School of Essential Ingredients
author: Erica Bauermeister
synopsis: Eight very different students gather in Lillian's restaurant kitchen every Monday night for a cooking class. Each carries a secret hunger beyond the culinary: a grieving widower, a young mother losing her identity, an Italian designer adrift in America. As Lillian guides them through dishes, she gently unlocks the deeper transformations each student needs to make.

reading_insights.reading_value: Comfort, community, and the healing power of food

title: Garden Spells
author: Sarah Addison Allen
synopsis: In the small town of Bascom, North Carolina, Claire Waverley tends a magical garden where edible flowers have secret powers. When her wild younger sister Sydney returns after a decade away — with a daughter and a dangerous past — the two must reconnect and confront their family's peculiar legacy, including an enchanted apple tree that throws fruit at anyone who gets too close.

reading_insights.reading_value: Escapism, charm, and the magic in everyday life

title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
author: Roald Dahl
synopsis: Poor Charlie Bucket wins one of five golden tickets granting entry to the mysterious Willy Wonka's chocolate factory. Inside, the wonders are extraordinary — but so are the dangers for greedy, gluttonous, or spoiled children. A darkly funny tale about virtue, imagination, and the purest kind of luck.

reading_insights.reading_value: Wonder, creativity, and the value of kindness

title: The Poppy War Trilogy
author: R.F. Kuang
synopsis: Fang Runin, a war orphan from rural China's fantasy analog, aces a national exam and enters the empire's elite military academy. She discovers she can channel the power of gods. But when war breaks out — inspired by the Sino-Japanese War and the Nanjing Massacre — Rin's rage and power spiral into something devastating, forcing her to confront what vengeance costs.

reading_insights.reading_value: Understanding trauma, war psychology, and the cost of power

title: Misery
author: Stephen King
synopsis: Novelist Paul Sheldon crashes his car in a blizzard and is 'rescued' by Annie Wilkes, his self-proclaimed number one fan. Trapped in her remote Colorado home with broken legs, Paul quickly realizes Annie is dangerously unhinged — and she wants him to resurrect the heroine he killed in his latest book, or else.

reading_insights.reading_value: Suspense, character psychology, and the power of storytelling

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
synopsis: On their fifth wedding anniversary, Nick Dunne's wife Amy disappears. As the police investigation zeroes in on Nick, alternating narratives reveal a marriage of lies, manipulation, and dark secrets — building to one of the most shocking twists in modern thriller fiction.

reading_insights.reading_value: Subverted expectations, gender dynamics, and manipulation

title: Dungeon Crawler Carl
author: Matt Dinniman
synopsis: When an alien corporation destroys Earth and converts it into an 18-level dungeon for an intergalactic reality show, Coast Guard vet Carl and his ex-girlfriend's insufferably regal cat Princess Donut must fight their way through monsters, traps, and drug-dealing llamas — while keeping their viewer ratings high enough to receive loot drops that might keep them alive.

reading_insights.reading_value: Pure entertainment, resilience, and dark catharsis

title: A Confederacy of Dunces
author: John Kennedy Toole
synopsis: Ignatius J. Reilly, an obese, flatulent, medievally opinionated slob living with his mother in New Orleans, is forced by circumstance to get a job. His resulting collision with the modern world — through a series of catastrophic misadventures — produces one of the funniest and most furiously satirical novels in American literature.

reading_insights.reading_value: Social satire, comic catharsis, and literary wit

title: The Anger Trap: Free Yourself from the Frustrations that Sabotage Your Life
author: Les Carter
synopsis: A clinical psychologist explores how unresolved anger silently sabotages relationships, work, and self-esteem. Carter identifies the patterns that keep people stuck in chronic frustration, offering practical strategies to recognize emotional triggers, understand the root causes of anger, and build healthier responses.

reading_insights.reading_value: Practical emotional intelligence and conflict resolution

title: Never Get Angry Again
author: David J. Lieberman
synopsis: Drawing on neuroscience, psychology, and spirituality, Lieberman argues that anger is always a choice rooted in ego and distorted thinking. He provides tools to permanently eliminate chronic anger by addressing its origins in low self-esteem, unmet expectations, and poor emotional habits.

reading_insights.reading_value: Emotional freedom and practical anger resolution

title: The Cow in the Parking Lot: A Zen Approach to Overcoming Anger
author: Leonard Scheff and Susan Edmiston
synopsis: A Buddhist-inspired guide that uses the thought experiment of imagining a cow in the parking lot — instead of a driver — to defuse rage and reframe frustration. It offers Zen philosophy and practical techniques to recognize irrational anger triggers and cultivate equanimity in daily life.

reading_insights.reading_value: Zen wisdom, humor, and emotional reframing

title: Facing the Fire: Experiencing and Expressing Anger Appropriately
author: John Lee
synopsis: John Lee argues that suppressed anger is at the root of depression, addiction, and toxic behavior. Drawing on his own recovery journey, he offers practical guidance on how to feel anger fully without exploding or imploding — learning to use it as a signal for healthy boundaries rather than a weapon.

title: Shigley's Mechanical Engineering Design
author: Richard Budynas and J. Keith Nisbett
synopsis: The definitive textbook for mechanical engineering design, covering the full design process from materials and load analysis to fatigue, deflection, shafts, bearings, gears, and fasteners. Combining fundamental theory with real-world industrial applications, it has been the standard reference for engineering students and professionals for over 50 years.

reading_insights.reading_value: Foundation for mechanical design practice and exam preparation

title: Fluid Mechanics: Fundamentals and Applications
author: Yunus A. Cengel and John M. Cimbala
synopsis: A comprehensive undergraduate textbook covering fluid statics, kinematics, conservation laws, viscous flow, turbomachinery, and compressible flow. Known for its accessible writing style, real-world examples, and problem sets that bridge fundamental theory with engineering applications across mechanical and civil disciplines.

reading_insights.reading_value: Core fluid dynamics knowledge for engineering practice

title: Steam Tables (Engineering Thermodynamics)
author: R.S. Khurmi
synopsis: A standard reference work covering thermodynamic properties of steam and water at various pressures and temperatures, widely used in mechanical and chemical engineering courses. Includes saturated and superheated steam tables, mollier diagrams, and supporting theory for thermodynamic cycle calculations.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Thermodynamic property lookup and cycle analysis
reading_insights.reading_value: Essential reference for thermal engineering calculations

title: Material Science and Engineering
author: R.K. Rajput
synopsis: A comprehensive textbook covering atomic structure, crystal defects, phase diagrams, mechanical and thermal properties of metals, ceramics, polymers, and composites. Designed for undergraduate engineering students, it bridges materials theory with practical applications in design and manufacturing.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Materials knowledge for design and manufacturing

title: Power Electronics
author: P.S. Bimbhra
synopsis: A leading textbook on power electronics covering thyristors, SCRs, power diodes, MOSFETs, IGBTs, AC/DC converters, inverters, choppers, and controllers. A standard reference for electrical engineering students preparing for examinations and professional practice in energy systems.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Circuit analysis and power conversion systems
reading_insights.reading_value: Core knowledge for electrical power systems engineering

title: Signals and Systems
author: A. Anand Kumar
synopsis: A comprehensive treatment of continuous and discrete-time signals, Fourier transforms, Laplace transforms, Z-transforms, and system analysis. A foundational textbook for electronics and communications engineering covering topics essential for signal processing, communications, and control systems.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Mathematical modeling and frequency domain thinking
reading_insights.reading_value: Foundational signal analysis for electronics engineers

title: Electric Machines
author: D.P. Kothari and I.J. Nagrath
synopsis: A comprehensive textbook on transformers, DC machines, induction motors, synchronous machines, and special machines. Widely used in electrical engineering programs, it combines theory with analysis of real machine behavior and is a standard reference for GATE and other competitive examinations.

reading_insights.reading_value: Core knowledge for electrical machines and drives

title: Optical Fiber Communications
author: Gerd Keiser
synopsis: The definitive reference on optical fiber communication systems, covering fiber optic theory, light propagation, optical sources, detectors, amplifiers, WDM systems, and network architectures. A classic graduate-level text widely used in telecommunications engineering programs worldwide.

reading_insights.reading_value: Foundation for modern optical communications engineering

title: Building Construction
author: B.C. Punmia
synopsis: A comprehensive civil engineering reference covering foundations, masonry, concrete construction, roofing, flooring, doors and windows, plastering, and advanced structural systems. A widely used textbook for civil engineering students preparing for degree examinations and professional practice.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Structural systems understanding and site knowledge
reading_insights.reading_value: Practical construction knowledge for civil engineers

title: Concrete Technology
author: M.S. Shetty
synopsis: An authoritative text on cement chemistry, concrete mix design, properties of fresh and hardened concrete, admixtures, durability, and special concretes including high-strength and fiber-reinforced types. A standard reference for civil engineering students and practicing professionals.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Materials knowledge for construction and structural design
reading_insights.reading_value: Authoritative reference for concrete practice and research

title: Civil Engineering: Conventional and Objective Type
author: R.S. Khurmi and J.K. Gupta
synopsis: A comprehensive examination guide covering all major topics in civil engineering including structural analysis, RCC design, fluid mechanics, soil mechanics, surveying, and highway engineering. Widely used by students preparing for GATE, IES, and state-level competitive examinations.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Exam readiness and comprehensive knowledge consolidation
reading_insights.reading_value: Targeted preparation for civil engineering competitive examinations

title: Advanced Engineering Mathematics
author: Erwin Kreyszig
synopsis: The most widely used mathematics textbook for engineering students, covering ODEs, linear algebra, vector calculus, Fourier analysis, partial differential equations, complex analysis, probability, and numerical methods. An encyclopedic reference that underpins all engineering disciplines.

reading_insights.reading_value: Essential mathematical foundation for all engineering fields

title: Vector Mechanics for Engineers
author: Beer, Johnston, Mazurek, and Cornwell
synopsis: A foundational textbook in statics and dynamics, covering equilibrium of rigid bodies, kinematics, kinetics, energy methods, and vibration. Known for its clear problem-solving methodology and extensive problem sets, it is a standard text in mechanical and civil engineering programs worldwide.

reading_insights.reading_value: Fundamental mechanics knowledge for design and analysis

title: Skunk Works: A Personal Memoir of My Years at Lockheed
author: Ben Rich
synopsis: The insider account of Lockheed's top-secret aerospace division, told by its chief, Ben Rich. From the U-2 spy plane to the SR-71 Blackbird to the F-117 stealth fighter, this is the true story of maverick engineers racing against Cold War pressures to build the most advanced aircraft in history — and the organizational genius that made it possible.

reading_insights.reading_value: Inspiration, engineering culture, and Cold War history

title: Debugging: The 9 Indispensable Rules for Finding Even the Most Elusive Software and Hardware Problems
author: David J. Agans
synopsis: A practical, entertainingly written guide that distills debugging into nine universal rules applicable to any hardware or software problem. Illustrated with engineering war stories, Agans teaches systematic thinking about failure — how to reproduce bugs, isolate variables, and track down even the most baffling problems with a cool, methodical head.

reading_insights.reading_value: Practical engineering mindset and debugging methodology

title: Principles: Life and Work
author: Ray Dalio
synopsis: Ray Dalio, founder of Bridgewater Associates, shares the unconventional principles he has used to build one of the world's largest hedge funds. Covering decision-making, idea meritocracy, radical transparency, and systematic thinking, the book argues that developing a set of explicit, testable principles is the key to excellence in work and life.

reading_insights.reading_value: Mental models, organizational design, and self-improvement

title: The Simple Wild
author: K.A. Tucker
synopsis: City girl Calla reconnects with her estranged father in the Alaskan bush, where bush pilot Jonah is infuriating, rugged, and everything she didn't expect. As the wild Alaskan landscape strips away her defenses, she must decide what she truly wants — and who she's willing to become.

reading_insights.reading_value: Escapism, emotional warmth, and romantic tension

title: Love and Other Words
author: Christina Lauren
synopsis: Macy and Elliot fell in love as teenagers sharing a secret reading nook filled with books — and then never spoke again. A decade later they collide in San Francisco. Told in alternating timelines of then and now, this is a story about first loves, silence, loss, and the words left unsaid.

reading_insights.reading_value: Love, loss, and the healing power of reconnection

title: November 9
author: Colleen Hoover
synopsis: Fallon and Ben meet by chance the day she's leaving LA for good. They agree to meet only one day each year — November 9 — and never in between. Each annual reunion brings new revelations and deeper connection, but a dark secret from the beginning threatens to destroy everything they've built.

reading_insights.reading_value: Emotional intensity and romantic drama with a twist

title: Every Summer After
author: Carley Fortune
synopsis: Percy and Sam spent five summers falling in love at a lakeside cottage in small-town Ontario. Then one night ended everything. Years later, a funeral brings Percy back — and forces her to face what she lost, what she did, and whether any of it can be rebuilt.

reading_insights.reading_value: Romantic longing, memory, and the courage to reconnect

title: A Thousand Boy Kisses
author: Tillie Cole
synopsis: Rune and Poppy fall in love as children and build a lifetime of memories in a jar of kisses. When Rune moves away and then returns years later, he finds Poppy keeping a devastating secret. A heartwrenching love story about the first and most consuming kind of love — and how some losses change you forever.

reading_insights.reading_value: Love, grief, and the weight of goodbye

title: The Gravity of Us
author: Brittainy C. Cherry
synopsis: Colin is a professional soccer player hiding a private storm of grief. Graham is a neighbor managing his own darkness. When their families collide, the two men find unexpected gravity in each other — a story about two broken people discovering they make each other whole.

reading_insights.reading_value: Healing through love and human connection

title: The Song of Achilles
author: Madeline Miller
synopsis: Patroclus, an awkward young prince, is exiled and meets the golden Achilles, son of a goddess and destined for eternal glory. Their bond deepens into love as the Trojan War approaches — a war that prophecy says Achilles must enter, and from which he will not return. A devastating retelling of the Iliad narrated by the man who loved Achilles most.

reading_insights.reading_value: Love, mortality, glory, and the meaning of sacrifice

title: A Little Life
author: Hanya Yanagihara
synopsis: Four friends move to New York after college: Willem, JB, Malcolm, and the brilliant but damaged Jude. Over decades, the novel follows their lives but circles relentlessly around Jude's hidden history of severe abuse and trauma. A profoundly difficult, profoundly beautiful novel about friendship, suffering, and what it means to love someone who cannot stop hurting.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Empathy, ethical inquiry, and emotional complexity
reading_insights.reading_value: Deep humanity, the limits of love, and the weight of the past

title: All Your Perfects
author: Colleen Hoover
synopsis: Quinn and Graham have a perfect life on paper — but years of infertility struggles have quietly hollowed out their marriage. Told in two timelines, the story moves between their falling in love and the painful present, asking whether love is enough to survive the grief of what cannot be fixed.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Emotional intelligence and empathy for complex relationships
reading_insights.reading_value: Honest look at love under pressure and marital resilience

title: Eleanor Oliphant Is Completely Fine
author: Gail Honeyman
synopsis: Eleanor Oliphant lives a rigidly solitary life, surviving on routine, vodka, and a burning secret. When she befriends an IT guy named Raymond and they help an elderly stranger together, the walls she has built around her devastating past begin to crack in this funny, tender, and ultimately heartbreaking novel about loneliness and healing.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Empathy, mental health awareness, and social understanding
reading_insights.reading_value: Loneliness, resilience, and the surprising forms of friendship

title: Normal People
author: Sally Rooney
synopsis: Connell and Marianne grow up in the same small Irish town but inhabit entirely different worlds. Their relationship shifts and reforms over the years — from high school secrecy to college intimacy and beyond — tracing how power, class, and communication failure shape even the most connected people.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Social dynamics, communication, and emotional intelligence
reading_insights.reading_value: Intimate character study and class consciousness in love

title: It Happened One Summer
author: Tessa Bailey
synopsis: LA socialite Piper Bellinger is sent to Westport, a tiny fishing town in Washington, as punishment. There she meets the gruff, gorgeous crab boat captain Brendan, who has no patience for Hollywood glitter. A fun, steamy opposites-attract romance with a big heart.

title: The Kiss Quotient
author: Helen Hoang
synopsis: Econometrist Stella Lane, who has autism and struggles with relationships, hires escort Michael Phan to teach her about love and intimacy. What starts as a business arrangement becomes something neither of them expected — a warm, sensory-rich romance that celebrates neurodiversity and the courage to be known.

reading_insights.reading_value: Representation, vulnerability, and the joy of genuine connection

title: Fourth Wing
author: Rebecca Yarros
synopsis: Violet Sorrengail is thrust into the brutal war college for dragon riders despite her fragile body. As she fights to survive alongside lethal classmates and bond with a dragon, her greatest threat might be the darkly magnetic Xaden Riorson — a rider with secrets that could destroy everything she's been taught to believe.

title: From Blood and Ash
author: Jennifer L. Armentrout
synopsis: Poppy is the Maiden — a sacred, untouchable figure kept isolated behind veils and strict rules. Her guard Hawke is forbidden, charming, and holds a devastating secret. As Poppy's sheltered world begins to crack, she discovers that everything she's been told about her destiny may be a lie.

title: The Cruel Prince
author: Holly Black
synopsis: Mortal girl Jude was taken to Faerie as a child. Now a teenager in the deadly fae court, she craves belonging and power in a world that views her as lesser. Her greatest antagonist is the cruel, beautiful Prince Cardan — and their hate-fueled rivalry will determine the fate of the entire faerie realm.

reading_insights.reading_value: Power dynamics, cunning heroines, and lush world-building

title: The House in the Cerulean Sea
author: TJ Klune
synopsis: Linus Baker is a by-the-books caseworker for magical children. He's sent to inspect a mysterious orphanage on a remote island — home to the most dangerous magical children imaginable, including the Antichrist. What he finds instead is found family, unexpected love, and a world that might be worth fighting for.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Emotional intelligence and value of acceptance
reading_insights.reading_value: Comfort, inclusivity, and the magic of found family

title: Legends & Lattes
author: Travis Baldree
synopsis: After decades of violence as an orc mercenary, Viv hangs up her sword to open the first coffee shop in a city that has never tasted the drink. A cozy, quietly revolutionary novel about chosen peace, small pleasures, and the surprising bravery of building something soft in a world built for hard things.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Mindfulness and appreciation for small joys
reading_insights.reading_value: Comfort, acceptance, and the radical act of choosing peace

title: Better Than the Movies
author: Lynn Painter
synopsis: Liz is a hopeless romantic obsessed with movie-perfect love. When her childhood nemesis Wes moves in next door, they make a reluctant pact: she helps him get the girl he likes; he helps her get closer to her dream guy Michael. Naturally, things do not go according to plan.

reading_insights.reading_value: Fun, warmth, and feel-good romantic comedy

title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before
author: Jenny Han
synopsis: Lara Jean Song Covey writes secret love letters to every boy she's ever loved — letters she never intends to send. When someone mails them all out, she has to face the consequences, including a fake-dating arrangement with one of the recipients that starts to feel dangerously real.

reading_insights.reading_value: Sweet romance, family love, and girlhood

title: Throne of Glass
author: Sarah J. Maas
synopsis: Celaena Sardothien, the world's most feared assassin, is offered a chance at freedom — if she can win the king's competition to become his royal champion. But the competition is far deadlier than it appears, and Celaena must navigate court intrigue, a growing mystery, and two men pulling her in opposite directions.

title: Powerless
author: Lauren Roberts
synopsis: In a kingdom where only the Gifted — those with powers — have worth, ordinary girl Paedyn is desperately hiding the fact that she has none. When she accidentally saves the life of a prince and is forced into the Purging Trials, she must use her wits alone to survive a competition designed to kill the powerless.

title: The Selection
author: Kiera Cass
synopsis: In a future dystopian America, thirty-five girls are chosen to compete for the heart of Prince Maxon in the Selection — part Bachelor competition, part fairy tale. America Singer wants nothing to do with it, but as she gets closer to the crown, she's no longer sure what she wants.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Light entertainment and female agency themes

title: Red Queen
author: Victoria Aveyard
synopsis: Mare Barrow is a lowly Red — one of the powerless masses ruled by silver-blooded elites with supernatural abilities. But when Mare discovers she possesses dangerous powers of her own, she's hidden in plain sight within the Silver court. A story of class revolution, deception, and the most dangerous game: trust.

reading_insights.cognitive_benefit: Social inequality awareness and political thinking
reading_insights.reading_value: Class dynamics, power, and the price of ambition
